section_path: Best Engineering Colleges in Pune for BTech
results.text:
Best Engineering Colleges in Pune for BTech
Choosing a good college is tough as several engineering colleges offer different courses. A student willing to get admission to top engineering colleges in Pune encounters the same problem. In Pune, there are a total of 129 engineering colleges. Out of these 129 colleges, nine are public or government, and 120 are private colleges. Choosing the right college out of these many colleges is a tough job. Among the best engineering colleges in Pune for BTech is MIT AOE.
Why choose BTech?
BTech is one of the most favored courses by science students with diverse fields such as Civil, Mechanical, Computer Science, Electrical, Information Technology, Electronics and Communication Engineering. Depending on your area of interest, the course offers candidates to hone their skills. No matter which area you pick, you are able to explore an ample amount of job opportunities after each technical programme, which gives you a promising profession.
The career options after BTech are much higher in managerial and technical fields. BTech graduates are also recruited as consultants, researchers, and subject matter experts. Any engineering graduate may hope to get a good job in the public or private sector abroad or in India itself.
Who can apply for BTech?
The basic eligibility standard for BTech is 12th standard with Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics. However, there are more criteria in every entrance exam and institute. Some of the institutes also give admission to their courses on a merit basis, i.e., based on class 12 board examination marks. For admissions, the most typical BTech entrance examinations are JEE Main and JEE Advanced. Students may need to appear for many state and private level entrance examinations to get into BTech courses at a college of their liking.
Best Engineering College in Pune for BTech - MIT AOE
MIT AOE (MIT Academy of Engineering), Alandi, Pune, was founded in 1999. The college offers seven different UG programs (B.Tech) and three PG programs (M.Tech). The programs are offered across significant engineering domains such as Civil, Mechanical, Chemical, Computer, Information Technology, Electronics and Telecommunication and Electronics.
Why go for BTech at MIT AOE?
MIT AOE is awarded ‘academic autonomy by the UGC (University Grants Commission). It is one of the top engineering colleges in Pune, with an ‘A’ grade accreditation from NAAC for all courses. There are many benefits of academic autonomy for all stakeholders of the institute.
Updated and contemporary syllabi; flexibility in revising as per industry needs.
Innovative strategies for teaching and learning; skill-based teaching.
Interdisciplinary practices in academics and research.
An extensive selection of subjects
Opportunities for collaborations with foreign & national universities and various companies.
Better placement options.
Support for higher education in India & abroad.
Internships and scholarships for projects.
Guest lectures and training programs by eminent experts from academia and industry.
Final Words
BTech offers much scope in today’s world. That’s why many high school students work towards getting into an engineering college after the 12th standard. The best way to be successful in this field, however, lies in the college you choose. With years of experience, excellent faculty, and a holistic syllabus, MIT AOE offers the platform you need to reach your career goals as an engineer. So, start your journey with one of the best engineering colleges in Pune for BTech - MIT AOE today.

Engineering needs lots of learning, understanding, and is considered one of the most challenging fields. B.Tech and M.Tech Courses offered for engineering students.
Accredited by NBA (National Board of Accreditation) and NAAC, also honored with the prestige of Academic Autonomy, MIT Academy of Engineering (MITAOE), is one of the best engineering colleges in Pune where students apply every year for admission. From B.tech and M.Tech courses to vibrant campus life and training programs, students here get opportunities for both personal & career growth. Courses offered in MIT Academy of Engineering (MITAOE)

B.Tech Second Year Admissions
ELIGIBILITY CRITERIA
DIRECT SECOND YEAR (LATERAL ENTRY) - FOR MAHARASHTRA
For Maharashtra State Candidature Candidate and All India Candidature Candidate.
The Candidate should be an Indian National.
Passed Diploma Course in Engineering and Technology with at least 45% marks (40% marks in case of candidates of Backward class categories and Persons with Disability belonging to Maharashtra State only) in appropriate branch of Engineering and Technology from an All India Council for Technical Education or Central or State Government approved Institution or its equivalent.
Passed B.Sc. Degree from a University Grants Commission (UGC) or Association of Indian Universities recognized University with at least 45% marks (40% in case of candidates of Backward class categories and Persons with Disability belonging to Maharashtra State only) and passed HSC with Mathematics as a subject Provided that students belonging to this category shall clear the subjects of Engineering Graphics/ Engineering Drawing and Engineering Mechanics of the first year Engineering Program along with second year subjects.
Any other criterion declared by appropriate authority from time to time.

All the rules about fees declared by the Government of Maharashtra – Fees Regulating Authority from time to time will be applicable to all the students. For Reserved Category students, Government rules will be followed and will be subject to the submission of all the required documents.

results.title: Best BTech Colleges in India | MIT AOE
results.text:
Best BTech Colleges in India
The Bachelor of Technology (B. Tech) is a four-year undergraduate programme divided into eight semesters. It is a professional engineering degree that is available in various fields, including Computer Science, Electronics and Communication, Information Technology, Electrical, Civil, and Mechanical Engineering.
The benefit of a B.TEch is that being a professional degree opens up many employment options in technical and management disciplines in both the public and commercial sectors in India and overseas. You might potentially be hired as a researcher, consultant, or other types of professional.
Various national, state and institute-level engineering entrance tests are held for admissions to B.Tech programmes to get into a top engineering college. One of the top Engineering colleges in Pune is MITAOE. Let us understand what makes it a distinct institute.
What Makes MITAOE One Of The Best B.Tech Colleges in India?
Here are some of the prominent reasons it attracts many students from all across the country.
Undergraduate Research Opportunities Program
As a freshman at MITAOE, you may work on cutting-edge research alongside some brightest minds. You'll be able to engage in a UROP, or Undergraduate Research Opportunities Program, at MITAOE. Professors at other colleges frequently accept only graduate students to assist them with their research.
It's also not difficult; 85 percent of students graduate with a UROP. An extra benefit is that you can get compensated for your efforts. That's a great deal. This makes MITAOE one of the best B.Tech colleges in India.
Resources
When you attend MITAOE, you will access the university's supplementary resources. They include free admission to the Museum, which has exhibitions, an Idea Hub, workshops, concerts, and more regarding STEAM issues and is situated on campus (Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts, Maths). Students and instructors often lead demonstrations.
Students can also get cheap tickets to the Museum of Fine Arts and the Boston Ballet, among other arts and cultural organizations. There are various choices for students who want to go about town, including campus shuttle services.
Placements
There is a 6 lakh package that is easily provided for B.tech civil engineer, structural engineer NSO on they offer many more degrees at the college such as civil, CSE, etc., mech and many more. Placements are the most important aspects and the kick-start to your career journey. Great, desirable packages make MITAOE one of the best B.Tech colleges in India.
Highly Competent Faculty
This college's professors are highly competent, with nearly every faculty member holding a PhD. They're all really helpful, and they're all very competent in their industries. The course curriculum is flexible and incorporates a lot of practical work and academics, giving students a thorough grasp of the topic and preparing them for the workplace.
As an autonomous institution, the curriculum is constantly revised to meet industry demands, and the placement ratio is good compared to other colleges.
Infrastructure
When you have to spend four years in an institution, infrastructure plays an important role. The hostel facilities in the institute are quite decent, and each room has its washroom. The Institute has a common area that is so clean and is used by students for group studies and parties. Institute also offer good-quality food to the students, which is rare in many institutes around the country. It provides students with a safe, healthy, and homely environment to thrive.
Closing
If you plan to get admission into an engineering college, MITAOE is the best choice for you. The one of a kind faculty, infrastructure, facilities and quality of education makes it the most desired private engineering college in India. The very affordable fee structure and placements are the cherries on the top.

results.title: M.Tech in Computer Engineering – MITAOE
results.text:
M.Tech in Computer Engineering – MITAOE
Lakhs of students sit for entrance exams every year to get into an esteemed engineering college. Engineering is one of the preferred courses as students get exposed to a world full of opportunities. Students can either take up a lucrative job or can go for higher studies like M.Tech in Computer Engineering or any preferred field. If a student wishes to pursue higher studies, it is best to opt for top engineering colleges in Pune, offering good technical education and placement guidance.
About M.Tech in Computer Engineering at MITAOE
MITAOE offers three PG courses, one of them being M.Tech in Computer Engineering. The two-year course helps the students to get in-depth knowledge in a Computer Science specialization. The entire curriculum is divided into six semesters, four semesters of theoretical and practical exams, while the last two semesters are allotted for project work.
The master's program makes the student shine in the competitive world and contributes more to the technological field.
Admission process for M.Tech
The number of intake for M.Tech Computer Engineering is 18. Every year, the admission procedures, courses, and forms are published by the Directorate of Technical Education. Candidates must fill-up the admission form and submit it before the deadline to get into a college.
To get into the M.Tech course, the student must have obtained a good score from the GATE exam with at least 50% in B.Tech. Other criteria are announced by the officials every year. All the rules and fee structure is declared every year by the Maharashtra government.
R&D department
MITAOE has an excellent research cell to help students with a platform to showcase their ideas. Students and faculty can use the R&D department to develop new technology. The R&D department was developed in 2008 to help the students work and explore the domain of Computer Engineering. The department helps the students channel their intellectual thoughts and energy to focus on researching new ideas and develop innovations.
Student council and clubs
Apart from a good curriculum, MITAOE also has a number of educational clubs, and the student committee also helps the students to grow more. Clubs like robotics and Hacker rank helps the students to keep learning and shaping their creative and intellectual ideas into reality.
Classroom programs, along with these educational clubs, help the students to love and understand the subject better.
Placement opportunities
MITAOE has a placement cell that assists and guides the students towards their dream career path. The college has tied-up with some international colleges so that interested students can choose to study abroad. The college also has an E-cell (entrepreneurial and development), which helps budding entrepreneurs realize their dreams of successfully creating a startup. The college has tie-ups with many industries and companies and helps the students in landing their dream job.

Home
Sports
Why AreSports Important in Education?

Sports are an essential component of well-rounded education since they engage the student, both physically and mentally.

School sports activities can develop discipline, teamwork, and a sense of persistence that helps students learn critical life skills that are important outside the academic world.

Virtually the minute you invest in student fitness programs, you invest in active youth, the intellectual development that comes from reducing stress, and health over time, something that is vital in this ever-changing educational world we live in.

Studies have shown that physical activity has a positive effect on cognitive development.

Therefore, students who are physically active have a positive impact on academic performance and sports as they are more likely to possess improved focus, memory, and problem-solving skills.

When students participate in sporting activities regularly, it builds confidence, leadership skills, social skills, and a sense of camaraderie among peers who support one another.

Schools that prioritise the importance of school sports help students become balanced individuals, capable of dealing with challenges with resolve and determination.

In addition to physical development, extracurricular sports programs educate one in patience, perseverance, and the process of accepting victory or defeat with grace.

Instilling these habits early sets the tone for individuals to have lifelong fitness, teambuilding, and personal growth habits so that they grow personally and emotionally to be able to tackle new challenges in life.

What Are the Benefits of Participating in Sports?

The role of sports in school has numerous positive outcomes that support personal, social, and academic growth.

Organised extracurricular sport activities help students to become healthier physically, more resilient in terms of endurance, and more coordinated, supplementing structured student fitness programs designed to ensure energy and vitality maintenance.

Sports also boost mental resistance, focus, and stress coping, consequently supporting enhanced academic and sporting performances.

From a social perspective, school sport events and activities offer opportunities for cooperation, communication, and leadership skills, creating opportunities for students to socialise and apply teamwork practices in real life with other students.

Engaging in sports also promotes skills like discipline, time management, and goal achievement, which in turn boost self-esteem and promote overall mental well-being.

In recognising the importance of school sports activities, academic institutions provide opportunities beyond academic attainment for students to flourish, supporting student well-being and satisfaction through concrete achievements.

The friendships, encouragement, and personal development that sports provide generate healthy competition, perspective, and perseverance to participate and face challenges, while providing balance and an active lifestyle.

Engaging in sport develops holistic skills that apply to all students’ lives as they transcend the educational experience and beyond.

How Are Sports Programs Conducted?(MIT AOE)

The MIT Academy of Engineering (MIT AOE) provides school sporting events and extracurricular programs that are intentional and sequenced to engage students and support their holistic learning and development.

The campus has great, well-resourced facilities to support exercise, such as basketball and volleyball courts, a football ground, a cricket ground, and a gymnasium, to facilitate student fitness programs.

MIT AOE offers inter-college competitions, wellness workshops and fitness challenges, where the value of sport in schools is communicated as an integral part of student life.

results.text:
The institute also has a sports center and sports ground where it offers different sports activities. The events such as “Nakshatra Sports day” is organized by MIT AOE where different colleges are invited for sports as well as cultural events. In such events, students can showcase their talent and skill.
In addition to this, national events such as Equilibrium, Datathon, Prakalp, Drone Race, Scicon, etc. are organized by the students club every year.
Experienced Faculty
A highly qualified and experienced team of faculty members are committed to providing quality education and mentorship during the course. All the faculty members have good hands-on industrial experience in addition to expertise in the relevant field. Faculty members actively take part in research activities and industrial collaboration to keep them updated with the latest technologies and trends.
Research and Innovation
Innovative and immersive research culture engages students in research activities all around the year. There are many research centers in the institute, including the Center for Research in Advanced Materials and the Center for Innovation and Entrepreneurship Development. To give academics and academicians a place to present their work and ideas, MIT AOE also conducts many national and international conferences, workshops, and seminars.

MIT AOE Library - Resources, Services, & Facilities
The Central Library at MITAOE preserves and provides access to a rich, diverse and enduring source of knowledge to inform, inspire and engage users in user intellectual and creative initiatives. It provides access to information, services and opportunities that enable, enrich and boost the quality of life for all its users.
The Central Library encourages readers by conducting various events throughout the year such as library orientation, various guest lectures for faculty, staff and students, demo presentation on e-books and various competitions like quiz, paper presentation, poster presentation, book and article review, debate, extempore through library week celebration to broaden their knowledge horizon and become more competent to face global challenges.

8. What is campus life like at MIT AOE?

results.title: Infrastructure and Facilities at MIT AOE: A Practical Overview for Students
section_path: Infrastructure and Facilities at MIT AOE: A Practical Overview for Students
results.text:
Infrastructure and Facilities at MIT AOE: A Practical Overview for Students
Infrastructure and Facilities at MIT AOE
When you first walk into the MIT Academy of Engineering, what you notice is not at all just the buildings.

It is the way everything feels ready for students.

For example, there are spaces where people hang around before class, and labs that smell faintly of machines and new wires.

Then there are quiet corners where you can catch up on notes.

Infrastructure and facilities shape how easy it is actually to get things done while you are here.

MIT AOE’s campus isn’t massive, but it’s well thought out.

Each building seems to have a clear job, so you’re not wandering for ages trying to find a lab or a classroom.

There’s usually a buzz between lectures, with students moving quickly from one block to another.

Even the walkways are planned so you do not waste time during busy days.

What infrastructure and facilities are available on campus?

Students here have access to proper campus facilities.

This covers everything from the classrooms you sit in every day to the labs you visit for practicals, the library, and a few simple places to grab food or study in groups.

There’s a dedicated building for each department, so it’s easy to find your teachers or lab supervisors.

The central library is bigger than you’d expect and stays pretty busy, especially before exams.

Labs are updated with what each department needs — you’ll see all the standard equipment, and nothing is outdated.

There are computer rooms with internet.

Hostels are available (though spots fill up quickly).

If you want a break, there’s a cafeteria and a few shaded areas outside that are good for quick chats or revision.

MIT AOE has also worked to keep things clean and safe.

You’ll find staff around most days making sure the equipment is working and the common areas are tidy.

How do facilities support student learning?

The academic infrastructure does what it should.

For example, classrooms are simple but have projectors and whiteboards.

And labs are scheduled so you actually get time to use the gear.

Group study is a real thing here — the library has dedicated spots for both solo learning and team work.

Teachers actively use technology in classrooms, going beyond traditional chalk-and-talk methods.

Assignments and projects become easier to manage with ready access to computers and printing facilities.

Student amenities like sports spaces and standard rooms give you a reason to stay back on campus instead of heading straight home after classes.

When the pressure ramps up, having good spaces makes it less stressful to finish significant assignments or prepare for finals.

Are research facilities included in the campus infrastructure?

Yes, research facilities are part of the setup.

Postgrads and final-year students use dedicated labs and project rooms for their research work.

Some departments have their own spaces with specialised equipment.

There is also access to online journals and a few technical publications in the library for anyone doing a project or looking to publish.

Why facilities matter at MIT AOE
At MIT AOE, attention to details means you do not have to scramble for basic things.

You get your lab time, you find the right teacher, and you’ve got a dedicated spot to study.

Modern classrooms and practical labs aren’t a luxury; they’re part of ensuring students actually learn what they’re supposed to.

When facilities work, students waste less time and can focus on the parts of uni that matter most.

Wrapping Up
Infrastructure at MIT AOE is smart and student-friendly.

NPTEL WEB AND VIDEO COURSES
NPTEL, National Program on Technology Enabled Learning, a project funded by the Ministry of Human Resource Development which is an initiative by all seven Indian Institutes of Technology (IITs) and Indian Institute of Science (IISc) which is established with a objective to make high quality learning material available to students of engineering institutions across the country by exploiting the advances in information and communication technology by developing curriculum based Video and Web Courses. The contents would be based on the model curriculum suggested by the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE).
Central Library has downloaded NPTEL web and video courses from the NPTEL web site and we have uploaded the same on the digital library server to save the time of users.
SWAYAM
SWAYAM platform is indigenously developed by Ministry of Human Resource Development (MHRD)and All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE) with the help of Microsoft. Swayam offers 2000 online e-learning courses and 80000 hours of learning. Central Library has started SWAYAM e-learning platform at MITAOE to promote e-learning from SWAYAM.
SWAYAM-NPTEL LOCAL CHAPTER
To promote e-learning, Central Library has established NPTEL Local Chapter at MITAOE to encourage students and faculty members to obtain online Certification through e-learning online certification Courses. MITAOE was awarded “A” and “AA” grade for SWAYAM-NPTEL Local Chapter and had secured a rank in top 50 and 100 Local Chapter respectively across India.
The National Digital Library (NDL) is an all-digital library that integrate all existing digitized and digital contents across Institutions of the nation to provide a single-window access with e-learning facility to different learning groups ranging from primary level to post graduation level and even life-long learners of our country. It is sponsored by MHRD and coordinated by IIT, Kharagpur. Central Library has subscribed the institutional membership of National Digital Library.

results.title: MIT AOE Library - QuillBot Information
results.text:
MIT AOE Library - QuillBot Information
QuillBot is an academic writing platform that can be used to improve the clarity and professionalism of your writing by rephrasing phrases and sentences and summarizing lengthy passages.
Steps to activate QuillBot Premium:
Visit https://quillbot.com and sign up using your institute E-Mail ID (@mitaoe.ac.in) and setup a password.
Click on the verification email you received to activate Premium.
Salient features of QuillBot:
Grammar Checker: Ensure accurate grammar, do spell check, verify punctuation, and rectify numerous grammatical problems. Additionally, there is a complimentary essay checker, a grammar checker, and various other tools available to enhance academic success.
Paraphrasing tool: The multilingual paraphrasing tool helps you rewrite and improve the clarity of any sentence, paragraph, or article.
Flow: An all-in-one writing space that combines all of QuillBot’s tools in one location with built-in grammar checking, allowing you to paraphrase, summarize, research, create citations, and write your essay without having to shift between tools.
Summarizer: QuillBot Summarizer can seamlessly condense content with precision using features like paragraph mode, key point extraction, and the ability to create custom modes for a tailored summarization experience.
Translator: QuillBot Translator simplifies cross-language communication, ensuring accuracy and clarity in diverse contexts, making language barriers a thing of the past.
AI Detector: Paste your text below to detect AI-generated content like ChatGPT, GPT-4, and Google Bard. QuillBot’s free AI detector will flag the text for further inspection and keep your writing human.
APA Citation Generator: Describe or evaluate your sources according to APA, MLA, Chicago, Harvard, etc., and let Scribbr’s Citation Generator find and fill in all the relevant information automatically.
QuillBot University: Register for live webinars, learn methods for enhancing your academic writing, productivity, and leverage tools to improve your writing and research skills.

results.title: MITAOE | Worshops on Ipr/Rm
results.text:
The lecture was attended by 470 faculties across pan India.

Dr. Ramesh Gaur explained importance of research data management in research process.

His talk was focused on how to create, organise, preserving and extracting the research data.

Research data management concerns about organisation of data from entry of research cycle to its archiving of results.

ACTIVITY REPORT 7 National Webinar on “Maximizing Visibility and Impact of Research output” Research and Development Cell in association with Central Library had organised a expert lecture on “Maximizing Visibility and Impact of Research output” for Faculty on 20/06/2020 at 5:00 pm.

The Speaker of for the webinar was Prof. Dr. Nabbi Hasan, IIT Delhi.

The lecture was attended by 431 faculties across pan India.

Dr Nabi Hasan spoke in detail about different research databases and indexing.

He discussed about Web of Science, Scopus and its transformation with time.

He shared information related to calculation of Citation index, H index, Impact factor and Cite score.

He shared the importance and methods of visibility of research.

ACTIVITY REPORT 8 National Level Webinar on “Publication Ethics and Predatory Journal” Research and Development Cell in association with Central Library had organised an expert lecture on “Publication Ethics and Predatory Journal” for Faculty on 23/06/2020 at 5:00 pm.

The Speaker of for the webinar was Dr. Shubada Joshi, Coordinator, Centre for Publication Ethics, Dept. of Library and Inf.

Sc., Savitribai Phule Pune University, Pune.

The lecture was attended by 474 faculties across pan India.

Prof. Dr. Shubada Joshi Ethics in research and various types of ethics.

She explained role of UGC in curbing plagiarism.

What steps UGC has taken to create awareness about ethics and plagiarism in research publications.

Dr Joshi spoke about different predatory journals, ways to find predatory journals and its effect on research.

ACTIVITY REPORT 9 Two Days Workshop on Patent Drafting.

On 29th and 30th September 2022, a workshop on patent drafting was jointly organized by IIC-MITAOE, R&D Cell and Central Library.

The aim of the workshop was to give step-by-step training to students on patent drafting.

21 project groups with patentable idea/prototype from different disciplines of engineering were identified.

These groups participated in the two days’ workshop.

The resource person mentored them during the entire

results.title: Research - MIT
results.text:
Research - MIT
R&D at MITAOE
Research is the extensive expansion of the existing stock of knowledge through high concentration of energy, effort and intellectual thought process that gives new dimensions to ideas, which produce far-reaching and everlasting results.

It is the pursuit of novelty with the help of study that includes observation, comparison, experiment, collecting and analyzing facts or data, and reaching desired conclusions either in the form of solution(s) towards the concerned problem or in certain generalizations for some theoretical modeling/formulation.

Precisely, the persistent quest for knowledge through objectives and systematic methods of finding solution(s) to a problem is research.

In Research and Development, one does research for the development of new techniques/ methods for inventing and improving a product as per the requirements of industry / society in terms of quality, cost, manpower, energy consumption, time etc.

To facilitate all the R & D activities in our institute among students and faculty/staff members, the R & D cell is functional and is being steered by the Director, Dean (R&D) and active representatives from each department.

A unique incentive increment scheme was introduced in 2008 for encouraging and boosting R&D culture in the institute.

Dr. Sunita Barve Professor, Deputy Director Academics and Research and Dean R&D, Email - dean.rnd@mitaoe.ac.in
Research Forefront Areas
Every school has identified three to four thrust areas where faculty members and students conduct research.

Each forefront area has Professors, Associate Professors, and Assistant Professors specialized in the respective areas.

ACTIVITY REPORT 3 Webinar on “Introduction to Turnitin Software” by Mr. Varun Research and Development Cell in association with Central Library had organised a expert lecture on “Introduction to Turnitin Software” for faculty on 28/04/2020 at 5:00 pm .

The Speaker for the webinar was Mr. Varun from Turnitin Software.

The lecture was attended by faculty of MITAOE.

In this webinar Mr Varun shared the step by step procedure of operating Turnitin software for plagiarism check.

He discussed in detail about similarity index, color coding for different sources.

He also discussed about self plagiarism, ramifications of keeping manuscript in repository, retrieval of manuscript from repository.

ACTIVITY REPORT 4 Nation level Webinar on Intellectual Property Rights Protection through Patent Research and Development Cell in association with Central Library had organised a expert lecture on “Intellectual Property Rights Protection through Patent” for Faculty on 06/06/2020 at 5:00 pm.

The Speaker of for the webinar was by Dr. Shri Ram, Nodal Officer, IPR Cell, Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology, Patiala, Punjab..

The lecture was attended by 223 faculties across pan India.

In this webinar Dr Shri Ram shared information of different types of Intellectual Property Rights like Patents, Copyrights and Trademarks.

He disseminated information related to different patent search agencies like WIPO, EPO, JPO, KIPO, SIPO, and USPTO.

In detail he explained different steps for national and international patent filing.

ACTIVITY REPORT 5 National level Webinar on “Devising Communication Strategy for Organization” Research and Development Cell in association with Central Library had organised an expert lecture on “Devising Communication Strategy for Organization” for Faculty on 13/06/2020 at 5:00 pm.

The Speaker of for the webinar was by Prof. Shantanu Ganguly, Fellow and Program Manager, Knowledge Resource Division, Project Management Unit, TERE, New Delhi.

The lecture was attended by 165 faculties across pan India.

In this webinar Dr. Shantanu Ganguly spoke about different aspects of knowledge Management like Knowledge Networking, KM Value Analysis, and Change management across different cultures globally.

He gave information related to various knowledge management systems, database management systems and devising communication strategy and content management.

ACTIVITY REPORT 6 National level Webinar on Research Data Management: Curation, Share, Use and Reuse”.

Research and Development Cell in association with Central Library had organised an expert lecture on “Research Data Management: Curation, Share, Use and Reuse” for Faculty on 17/06/2020 at 5:00 pm.

The Speaker of for the webinar was Prof. Dr. Ramesh Gaur, Dean and Director, Kala Nidhi Division, IGNCA, Ministry of Culture, Govt of India, New Delhi.

The lecture was attended by 470 faculties across pan India.

Dr. Ramesh Gaur explained importance of research data management in research process.

His talk was focused on how to create, organise, preserving and extracting the research data.

MITAOE
Spiritual Mind Club
The 'Spiritual Minds Club' focuses on physical and mental well-being through activities like yoga, Pranayama, spiritual discussions, and promoting ethical values. Yoga exercises enhance physical fitness, mental clarity, and inner peace. Pranayama teaches stress management and emotional resilience. Thoughtful discussions encourage introspection and spiritual growth. Emphasis on ethical values enriches character and interpersonal relationships.
At the core of our mission is the dedication to fostering the well-rounded development of MITAOE students. We want them to look back and see that their MITAOE experience went beyond engineering and instilled profound spiritual values.
In summary, the 'Spiritual Minds Club' aims to guide MITAOE students towards holistic development, shaping them not only as engineers but as individuals with strong spiritual values."

results.title: MITAOE | ROTARY CLUB
results.text:
MITAOE
ROTARY CLUB
WHAT IS ROTARY ?
Paul Harris founder of Rotary Club, founded in the Chicago (United States) on Feb. 23, 1905. Rotary promote health, peace and prosperity in communities across the globe. It unites people from all continents and cultures who take action to deliver real long term solutions to our world’s most persistent issues.
Rotary is made up of three parts :-
I. Local Clubs
II. Rotary International
III. The Rotary Foundaion
What is ROTARACT ?
Rotaract club brings together people from the age 18 and older to exchange their ideas with leaders in the community, it develops leadership qualities and professionalism. In communities worldwide, Rotary and Rotaract members work side by side to take action through service.
SOME GLIMPSES OF ROTARACT CLUB :-
Parent Club RC Pimpri ELITE
AGENDA OF ROTARACT
Rotary club Meetings are held weekly with focus being on fellowship. That said, there are plenty of news updates and discussion that every member should be kept aware of, and a great way to organiz meetings is with a formal Rotary agenda.
BENEFITS OF ROTARACT FOR MITAOE
This is the first year of Rotaract in MITAOE, as it is an International Organization, there are many benefits for student members.
Personality Growth
Good communication skills
Increase in self confidence
Social interaction
Placement opportunities
Economic knowledge
Leadership Qualities
Interaction with many business tycoons and many industrialists
Decision Making Power
Internship opportunities
FIRST PROGRAM CONDUCTED IN MITAOE
On 21st of April 2022, Rotary Club of Pimpri Elite organized session on “Introduction to Rotary Club and it’s benefits as an Student Engagement Program”. Around 70 Students have participated in the session from various streams and classes across. Session was taken by Mr. Ashok D. Shinde (Secretary of Rotary Club of Pimpri Elite). The Introductory speech was given by Mr.Ravindra Bhave Sir (President of Rotary Club of Pimpri Elite). Faculty coordinator Dr . N.B.Totla hosted and coordinated this session.
CONSIDERATION
We have received Rotaract Club of MIT Academy of Engineering consideration, and is now a member of ROTARY INTERNATIONAL Which is sponsored by Rotary Club of Pimpri Elite.
CERTIFICATE OF ORGANIZATION
TENTATIVE INSTALLATION CEREMONY DATE
By considering over all conditions Installation Ceremony for Rotaract Club of MIT Academy of Engineering will be conducted in first /second week of August .
REPORT ON STUDENT ENGAGEMENT PROGRAM

At MITAOE, we are committed to fostering an inclusive and dynamic environment where every student has the opportunity to learn, grow, and make a meaningful impact. Our student clubs play a central role in this mission by offering a wide range of engaging activities designed to enhance both personal and academic development.
From hands-on workshops and insightful seminars to lively social events and impactful community outreach programs, MITAOE's student clubs provide an array of experiences that cater to diverse interests. These initiatives are designed to help students cultivate essential skills, expand their knowledge, and build lasting connections.
Goal
Our goal is to create a safe, healthy, and supportive atmosphere where students can thrive in all aspects of their lives. At MITAOE, we believe in nurturing not just intellectual growth, but also the technical, physical, emotional, and ethical development of our students. By promoting a holistic approach, we aim to help our students become well-rounded individuals who are prepared to contribute meaningfully to society and their communities

results.title: MITAOE | Autosports
results.text:
MITAOE
Autosports
TEAM NIYUDRATH RACING (TNR) - AUTOSPORTS CLUB
Team Niyudrath racing, a club under the autosports club, aims at designing, manufacturing and testing an ATV to participate in various national level events like SAE BAJA, Enduro Student India (ESI), etc. For this we prepare by creating a team capable of handling the immense pressure of deadlines, submissions, academics, failures, etc. whilst staying together in all these situations. As a contribution toward the college, we take workshops every year that are open for everyone and teach them about our ATV and also different components that are being used in the car. Most of the people inspired by this, attend the recruitment process of the team. After screening and becoming part of the team, they get exposed to a professional working environment where they are exposed to seniors, the actual mechanical market, professional skills, etc. Our work is distributed among the following departments – Brakes, CAE, Powertrain, Roll cage, Steering, Suspension. All these departments work in unison to complete our stages which are design on the basis of which we are judged which decides which SAE BAJA event we would be attending. manufacturing, testing and finally the event
In our legacy of EIGHT years and after EIGHT significant variants of our custom- built ATV whom we like to call the ENX, we have managed to secure some awards for the team and the college

Sports Club
MIT MASTER BLASTERS
“Do you know what my favorite part of the game is? The opportunity to play.” -Mike Singletary
The sports club's name is MIT MASTER BLASTER, and it's here to provide students with a passion for sports a voice. The club's mission is to instil in kids a sense of sportsmanship and a love of competition, as well as the value of time, precision, and teamwork.
As part of its mission, the organisation focuses on instilling and developing the sports culture among students and faculty members to foster collaboration, coordination among varied cultural and ethnic groups, and the infusion of discipline and values into one individual.
Mr.Atul Waghmare, Sports Director adwaghmare@admin.maepune.ac.in (9527534822)
Mr.Kartik Kurhade, sports assistant.
OBJECTIVES
To provide a platform to talented and interested students in sports
To develop sports skills and sportsman spirit in students.
To make students understand, value of time, precision & competitiveness along with communication, coordination & teamwork.
To inculcate and promote the sports culture amongst the students and faculty to learn teamwork at work, coordination among diverse cultural & ethnic groups and mainly infuses discipline & instills the value system in one individual.
SPORTS FACILITIES
Outdoor sports: Club have outdoor sports ground of about 50 by 70 yards to organize various sports like cricket, football, volleyball, basketball, kabaddi etc.
Indoor sports: Club have spacious indoor game room of about 30 by 30 yards. Students practice and play table tennis, chess and carom without any disturbance. We have got 3 new table tennis tables from the grand sanction by Pune University.
New ground: Club has new ground of area 180*60 meters square where student practice football cricket, kho-kho matches.
SALIENT FEATURES
Students extra effort in sports improves levels of physical fitness and it ensures a sound state of mind.
Sports club teaches students the leadership qualities and overall discipline as well as moral ethics and social awareness.
Sport club is the perfect place for dealing with loss in-game and equally important is teaching students to win compassionately.
A student’s involvement in sports club activities in educational life creates a spirit of higher achievements eventually.

results.title: MITAOE | Spark Club
results.text:
MITAOE
Spark Club
This club provides a platform for all techies to work on different projects. This will create a technical awareness among students, to brings out the passion in youngsters and develop their organizational and technical skills, Improve the quality of students through learning, Make them capable individuals ready to explore beyond the horizon and Instill in them an inquisitive learning nature.
Here you will get an opportunity to build networks with industries and interact with industry experts. The principle reason behind this club is to improve your grasp on hardware electronics. This is a community where you can learn and work together on shared goal.
This club brings out a good balance in work, fun and leisure activities and ensures the all-round development of students.
BENEFITS OF CLUB:
This club will enrich your experience, create a diverse community, encourage involvement, and provide opportunities for students to build valuable skills.
This helps you to build formal and informal networks while exploring career opportunities.
Here students will get an opportunity to enhance new skills by working on different projects.
Open to all students, these clubs organize programs, events, and meetings throughout the academic year.

Get hands on experience by making projects.
Conducting technical events such as Technophilia, etc.
Industry Visits to provide students an insight regarding internal working of companies.
Query solving sessions

results.title: MITAOE | Literary Club
results.text:
MITAOE
Literary Club
The world out there judges your comprehensive skills which do not just end at technical. We, at literary club, believe that we can inculcate and shape interpersonal skills in students through innovative activities, competitions and workshops. Thus, helping in holistic development of every seeker. The events organized cover every aspect of: Listening, Speaking, Reading, and Writing skills. Along with this, we also help ignite the passion of fandom subculture through quizzes and day celebrations. Oh, did we mention we are also the 2nd best non-technical club of our college.
EVENTS
Inter-college as well as inter-college organized offline as well as online : Debate, Group Discussion, Lecturette, Elocution, Open Mic.
Lit Week: A week filled with celebration of various fandom subcultures like Harry Potter, Anime, Superheroes, Web series, etc with exciting prizes to win.
Club Mela: Out-of-the-box activities like wordrubiks cube, heads up, word dice, etc.
Commemoration of regional languages (Hindi, Marathi): काव्यांजलि (Hindi), और बताओ, आगे क्या होगा? (Hindi), स्लोगन प्रतियोगिता (Hindi), पोवाडा पठण (Marathi), निबंध लेखन (Marathi), etc.
GOT Day: A quiz battle
Workshops on Creative Writing, Technical Writing by respective field experts.
Madlib Theatre, Spell bee, Turncoat, PPDT, etc
Other than all this, we have an engaging social media with fun pursuits where we post quizzes, quotes, movie and book recommendations, grammar and pronunciation tips, etc.

results.title: Student Clubs - MIT
results.text:
Student Clubs - MIT
At MITAOE, we are committed to fostering an inclusive and dynamic environment where every student has the opportunity to learn, grow, and make a meaningful impact. Our student clubs play a central role in this mission by offering a wide range of engaging activities designed to enhance both personal and academic development.
From hands-on workshops and insightful seminars to lively social events and impactful community outreach programs, MITAOE's student clubs provide an array of experiences that cater to diverse interests. These initiatives are designed to help students cultivate essential skills, expand their knowledge, and build lasting connections.
Goal
Our goal is to create a safe, healthy, and supportive atmosphere where students can thrive in all aspects of their lives. At MITAOE, we believe in nurturing not just intellectual growth, but also the technical, physical, emotional, and ethical development of our students. By promoting a holistic approach, we aim to help our students become well-rounded individuals who are prepared to contribute meaningfully to society and their communities

results.text:
The lecture was attended by faculty of MITAOE.

In this webinar Mr. Adnan discussed the importance of plagiarism.

He gave a walk through to the entire Turnitin software.

He explained in detail various features of the software besides plagiarism check.

ACTIVITY REPORT 3 Webinar on “Introduction to Turnitin Software” by Mr. Varun Research and Development Cell in association with Central Library had organised a expert lecture on “Introduction to Turnitin Software” for faculty on 28/04/2020 at 5:00 pm .

The Speaker for the webinar was Mr. Varun from Turnitin Software.

The lecture was attended by faculty of MITAOE.

In this webinar Mr Varun shared the step by step procedure of operating Turnitin software for plagiarism check.

He discussed in detail about similarity index, color coding for different sources.

He also discussed about self plagiarism, ramifications of keeping manuscript in repository, retrieval of manuscript from repository.

ACTIVITY REPORT 4 Nation level Webinar on Intellectual Property Rights Protection through Patent Research and Development Cell in association with Central Library had organised a expert lecture on “Intellectual Property Rights Protection through Patent” for Faculty on 06/06/2020 at 5:00 pm.

The Speaker of for the webinar was by Dr. Shri Ram, Nodal Officer, IPR Cell, Thapar Institute of Engineering and Technology, Patiala, Punjab..

The lecture was attended by 223 faculties across pan India.

In this webinar Dr Shri Ram shared information of different types of Intellectual Property Rights like Patents, Copyrights and Trademarks.

He disseminated information related to different patent search agencies like WIPO, EPO, JPO, KIPO, SIPO, and USPTO.

In detail he explained different steps for national and international patent filing.

ACTIVITY REPORT 5 National level Webinar on “Devising Communication Strategy for Organization” Research and Development Cell in association with Central Library had organised an expert lecture on “Devising Communication Strategy for Organization” for Faculty on 13/06/2020 at 5:00 pm.

The Speaker of for the webinar was by Prof. Shantanu Ganguly, Fellow and Program Manager, Knowledge Resource Division, Project Management Unit, TERE, New Delhi.

The lecture was attended by 165 faculties across pan India.

In this webinar Dr. Shantanu Ganguly spoke about different aspects of knowledge Management like Knowledge Networking, KM Value Analysis, and Change management across different cultures globally.

He gave information related to various knowledge management systems, database management systems and devising communication strategy and content management.

ACTIVITY REPORT 6 National level Webinar on Research Data Management: Curation, Share, Use and Reuse”.

Research and Development Cell in association with Central Library had organised an expert lecture on “Research Data Management: Curation, Share, Use and Reuse” for Faculty on 17/06/2020 at 5:00 pm.

The Speaker of for the webinar was Prof. Dr. Ramesh Gaur, Dean and Director, Kala Nidhi Division, IGNCA, Ministry of Culture, Govt of India, New Delhi.

The lecture was attended by 470 faculties across pan India.

Dr. Ramesh Gaur explained importance of research data management in research process.

His talk was focused on how to create, organise, preserving and extracting the research data.

results.title: Infrastructure and Facilities at MIT AOE: A Practical Overview for Students
section_path: Infrastructure and Facilities at MIT AOE: A Practical Overview for Students
results.text:
Infrastructure and Facilities at MIT AOE: A Practical Overview for Students
Infrastructure and Facilities at MIT AOE
When you first walk into the MIT Academy of Engineering, what you notice is not at all just the buildings.

It is the way everything feels ready for students.

For example, there are spaces where people hang around before class, and labs that smell faintly of machines and new wires.

Then there are quiet corners where you can catch up on notes.

Infrastructure and facilities shape how easy it is actually to get things done while you are here.

MIT AOE’s campus isn’t massive, but it’s well thought out.

Each building seems to have a clear job, so you’re not wandering for ages trying to find a lab or a classroom.

There’s usually a buzz between lectures, with students moving quickly from one block to another.

Even the walkways are planned so you do not waste time during busy days.

What infrastructure and facilities are available on campus?

Students here have access to proper campus facilities.

This covers everything from the classrooms you sit in every day to the labs you visit for practicals, the library, and a few simple places to grab food or study in groups.

There’s a dedicated building for each department, so it’s easy to find your teachers or lab supervisors.

The central library is bigger than you’d expect and stays pretty busy, especially before exams.

Labs are updated with what each department needs — you’ll see all the standard equipment, and nothing is outdated.

There are computer rooms with internet.

Hostels are available (though spots fill up quickly).

If you want a break, there’s a cafeteria and a few shaded areas outside that are good for quick chats or revision.

MIT AOE has also worked to keep things clean and safe.

You’ll find staff around most days making sure the equipment is working and the common areas are tidy.

How do facilities support student learning?

The academic infrastructure does what it should.

For example, classrooms are simple but have projectors and whiteboards.

And labs are scheduled so you actually get time to use the gear.

Group study is a real thing here — the library has dedicated spots for both solo learning and team work.

Teachers actively use technology in classrooms, going beyond traditional chalk-and-talk methods.

Assignments and projects become easier to manage with ready access to computers and printing facilities.

Student amenities like sports spaces and standard rooms give you a reason to stay back on campus instead of heading straight home after classes.

When the pressure ramps up, having good spaces makes it less stressful to finish significant assignments or prepare for finals.

Are research facilities included in the campus infrastructure?

Yes, research facilities are part of the setup.

Postgrads and final-year students use dedicated labs and project rooms for their research work.

Some departments have their own spaces with specialised equipment.

There is also access to online journals and a few technical publications in the library for anyone doing a project or looking to publish.

Why facilities matter at MIT AOE
At MIT AOE, attention to details means you do not have to scramble for basic things.

You get your lab time, you find the right teacher, and you’ve got a dedicated spot to study.

Modern classrooms and practical labs aren’t a luxury; they’re part of ensuring students actually learn what they’re supposed to.

When facilities work, students waste less time and can focus on the parts of uni that matter most.

Wrapping Up
Infrastructure at MIT AOE is smart and student-friendly.

Sophisticated laboratories, fully equipped workshops, and collaborative areas facilitate cutting-edge research techniques, such as simulation, prototyping, and applied experimentation.

MIT AOE promotes scholarly research that complements industry requirements, developing internships, live projects, and research publications.

The integration of global inputs, industrial experience, and ethical conduct enables students to develop critical thinking and technical skills.

MIT Academy of Engineering
The MIT Academy of Engineering (MIT AOE) is a well-known autonomous engineering college in the Alandi neighborhood of Pune. The institute, which was founded in 1999, has emerged as a pioneer in the area of engineering education and research. MIT AOE has consistently been ranked among the top engineering colleges in Pune and India, with a strong emphasis on innovation, industry collaborations, and interdisciplinary learning.
In this blog, we are going to discuss what makes MIT AOE an excellent college of engineering.
Affiliation and Accreditations
MIT AOE is an autonomous college affiliated with SavitriBai Phule University (SPPU).
The institute is approved by the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE).
It has also got approval from the Government of Maharashtra.
The institute is accredited by NAAC with an ‘A’ grade for all programs.
In addition, the institute should also have the 2(F) & 12(B) status of the UGC.
The institute has made a name for itself through academic and research excellence, and it continues to expand as a 'Centre of Excellence' in engineering education and research.
It has ranked 1st in Maharashtra & 11th in India amongst the finest Private Engineering Colleges by Outlook Survey.
Why is MIT AOE the Best Autonomous Engineering College in Pune?

Industry Oriented Curriculum
The curriculum at MIT AOE is designed to provide students with a well-rounded education that includes theoretical knowledge as well as practical experience. Mechanical Engineering, Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering, Computer Engineering, Chemical Technology, and Civil Engineering are among the undergraduate programs available at the college. The courses are updated regularly to reflect the most recent industry trends and technologies.

results.title: Top Engineering Programs - MIT Academy of Engineering
section_path: Top Engineering Programs - MIT Academy of Engineering
results.text: result, skilled civil engineers are in high demand across industries and Government sectors. The department offers a pool of highly skilled and experienced faculty members that encourage students to think, plan, design, construct, and maintain infrastructure innovatively. Moreover, the students could learn various inter-disciplinary computer skill courses like IoT, AI/ML, Data science, computer security, etc. They are exposed to learning software popular in Civil engineering Industries. We support students to develop their technical and soft skills to ensure their overall development. Our state-of-the-art classrooms and well-equipped laboratories promote effective teaching and learning. We provide civil engineers who can take the head-on challenge to build a better world. Dr. Nilesh. U. Mate, Professor & HOD, Department of Civil Engineering Contact Number – 020-30253500 Ext. 1261 Email – hodcivil@mitaoe.ac.in Department of Chemical Engineering ( NBA ACCREDITED ) The Department of Chemical Engineering was established in the year 1999 with intake of 60 students. The course syllabus of the Department has been designed by keeping in mind the Chemical Engineering syllabi of various international institutions. The course syllabus contains a combination of theory courses as well as laboratory experimentation. This inculcates a habit of relating observations to fundamentals. In addition, the students spend six –eight weeks in industry during their vacations, so as to update themselves with real life industrial situations. In doing so, they get an opportunity to apply theory to real –life problems. The students are also made to do independent projects, seminars and home papers. This develops their independent thinking and creative ability. It helps them to analyze a given problem through various angles and synthesize innovative engineering solutions. The undergraduate program of Chemical Engineering is accredited four times ( Three times in Tier-II & one time in Tier-I ) by National Board of Accreditation (NBA). Department is well known for its student centric activity via various events like seminar, conferences, workshops, guest lectures, industrial visits, add-on courses etc. conducted throughout the year to enrich the students’ knowledge and contribute towards bridging the gap between the curriculum and industrial requirement. The students graduating from the Department of Chemical Engineering are well placed in profession both in India and abroad. Also, a good number of students pursue higher studies in India as well as in other countries like USA, Europe. IIChE student chapter of Department of Chemical Engineering is honored with “Pidilite Industries Best Student Chapter Award” for the year 2013 for being adjudged the Best Student Chapter among all other student chapter under Indian Institute of Chemical Engineers. SCHEMCON – 2015 was successfully organized by the chapter in September 2015 on the theme ‘Sustainable Development for Better Tomorrow’. It was for the first time in western region. The Ignited Minds - Club conducts social, cultural and technical events for the students and club members alike. It organizes socio-cultural events and also strives to raise awareness of Indian culture in the surrounding regions through lectures and orientations. These activities help in nourishing qualities like organizing skills, leadership quality, social awareness, entrepreneurship quality among students. Thus, we provide a vibrant and creative learning environment for our students. We invite you to explore more about our Department of Chemical Engineering through this website, and also invite you to personally visit our Department to learn more! Dr. S. P. Shewale, Associate Professor & HOD, Department of Chemical Engineering Phone : 020-30253615/ 30253500 Ext. 1045 Email: hodchem@mitaoe.ac.in

results.title: Top Engineering Programs - MIT Academy of Engineering
section_path: Top Engineering Programs - MIT Academy of Engineering
results.text:
We are entering a brand-new era of software engineering as more CIOs and digital leaders recognise the value of this field and the potential effects it may have on your bottom line.

It's vital that vendors, IT personnel, and even departments outside of IT be aware of how software engineering is affecting nearly every facet of your daily business operations.

The fact that software developers create things that didn't previously exist is what makes them so valuable in this area of expertise.

This discipline is unique because of the fine line it strikes between creativity and science.

They must be able to visualise the finished result before they can create it.

Do you want to create software that meets user requirements?

The answer to this query will greatly influence how your engineers operate.

Those who invest in software engineering now will reap the rewards for many years to come, and there may be even more alternatives in the future.

There are many excellent job openings right now in all kinds of sectors.

Dr Manish Giri, HOD, Department of Computer Engineering (Software Engineering) Email – hod.softengg@mitaoe.edu.in Department of Computer Science & Engineering (Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning)

The Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning domain develops intelligent, adaptive systems using machine learning, deep learning, and generative AI.

It emphasizes problem-solving, innovation, and applications across industries like healthcare, robotics, and finance, preparing professionals for opportunities in cutting-edge, emerging technologies.

Dr. Diptee Ghusse(Chikmurge), HOD, Computer Science & Engineering (Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning) Email – hod.aiml@mitaoe.edu.in Department of Computer Science & Engineering (Data Science)

Data Science is an interdisciplinary field that leverages scientific methods, algorithms, and systems to extract insights and knowledge from structured and unstructured data.

It integrates techniques from statistics, computer science, and domain expertise to analyse and interpret complex data.

Dr. V.C.Wangikar, HOD, Computer Science & Engineering (Data Science) Email – hod.datasci@mitaoe.edu.in Department of Information Technology The Information Technology program equips students with the skills and knowledge necessary to thrive in the fast-paced tech industry.

Focusing on areas such as software development, data management, cybersecurity, and networking, the program prepares graduates for diverse IT roles across various sectors.

Students gain hands-on experience with the latest technologies, fostering problem-solving and innovation.

The curriculum emphasizes critical thinking, project management, and industry-specific applications, ensuring graduates are ready to meet the challenges of the ever-evolving digital landscape.

The program is designed to develop versatile IT professionals capable of addressing global technological challenges and advancing in their careers.

Dr. Santosh Warpe, HOD, Information Technology Email – hod.it@mitaoe.edu.in Department of Mechanical Engineering ( NBA ACCREDITED )

The Mechanical Engineering curriculum includes various hands-on skill courses such as Industrial Measurement & Instrumentation Computer-Aided Product Design, Mechanical Simulation, Object Oriented Programming, etc.

It provides opportunities for students to acquire interdisciplinary skills and knowledge through various Choice-Based Curriculum System courses (CBCS).

Mechanical students can complete the specialisation in Computer-Aided Engineering, Robotics & Automation, and Automobile Engineering.

Many industry experts from various renowned industries deliver the skill courses to give practical flavor to learning.

Students actively file the patent, start their ventures, and present technical papers at the international conference during their academics.

results.title: Top Engineering Programs - MIT Academy of Engineering
section_path: Top Engineering Programs - MIT Academy of Engineering
results.text:
Top Engineering Programs - MIT Academy of Engineering Undergraduate Programs Post Graduate Programs PHD Programs Duration : 4 Years B.Tech Chemical Engineering

The course syllabus of the Department has been designed by keeping in mind the Chemical Engineering syllabi of various international institutions.

The course syllabus contains a combination of theory courses as well as laboratory experimentation.

This inculcates a habit of relating observations to fundamentals.

In addition, the students spend six –eight weeks in industry during their vacations, so as to update themselves with real life industrial situations.

In doing so, they get an opportunity to apply theory to real –life problems.

The students are also made to do independent projects, seminars and home papers.

This develops their independent thinking and creative ability.

It helps them to analyze a given problem through various angles and synthesize innovative engineering solutions.

Duration : 4 Years B.Tech Civil Engineering The Department of Civil Engineering, established in 2014-15 with an intake of 60 students, offers a comprehensive curriculum inspired by international standards, combining theoretical courses with hands-on laboratory experimentation.

Duration : 4 Years B.Tech Computer Engineering Global Excellence in research, teaching and technology development is the vision of the Department.

The sustained effort is to produce computing graduates with potential to design and develop innovative approaches to programming and problem solving to meet the needs of knowledge era.

Department runs Two UG program ( Computer Engineering ) and One PG Program, to meet the demand for well-qualified software professionals.

Duration : 4 Years B.Tech Computer Engineering (Software Engineering)

The Software Engineering program combines computer science principles with software engineering practices to prepare students for a successful career in developing high-quality software solutions.

The program focuses on software development, system architecture, programming, testing, and project management, emphasizing both technical expertise and problem-solving abilities.

Students will learn to design, develop, and maintain scalable software systems while applying industry best practices in software lifecycle management.

The curriculum is designed to provide a solid foundation in computer science while fostering creativity and innovation in software engineering.

Graduates will be well-prepared to meet the growing demand for skilled software engineers in various sectors.

Duration : 4 Years B.Tech Computer Science & Engineering (Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning)

The Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning domain develops intelligent, adaptive systems using machine learning, deep learning, and generative AI.

It emphasizes problem-solving, innovation, and applications across industries like healthcare, robotics, and finance, preparing professionals for opportunities in cutting-edge, emerging technologies.

Duration : 4 Years B.Tech Computer Science & Engineering (Data Science)

Data Science is an interdisciplinary field that leverages scientific methods, algorithms, and systems to extract insights and knowledge from structured and unstructured data.

It integrates techniques from statistics, computer science, and domain expertise to analyse and interpret complex data.

Duration : 4 Years B.Tech Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering The Department of E&TC Engineering, established in 1999 with an intake of 180 students, offers a comprehensive curriculum inspired by international standards, combining theoretical courses with hands-on laboratory experimentation bridging the gap between hardware and software concepts Duration : 4 Years B.Tech Information Technology The Information Technology program equips students with the skills and knowledge necessary to thrive in the fast-paced tech industry.

Focusing on areas such as software development, data management, cybersecurity, and networking, the program prepares graduates for diverse IT roles across various sectors.

Dr. Shitalkumar Jain Group Director

-Placement and Corporate Relations
MITAOE is a highly regarded campus for leading multinational corporations seeking talent through campus placements.

The Corporate Relations and Placement Cell boast a legacy of facilitating top-notch placements for students, with our alumni making significant contributions across various sectors globally.

As a result, industry leaders frequent MITAOE to scout for engineers with innovative and reengineering prowess.

The Corporate Relations and Placement Cell fosters a robust Corporate Interface by organizing interactive sessions with industry veterans and providing soft-skills training to groom students for the corporate world.

It is committed to offering exceptional job opportunities and nurturing entrepreneurial aspirations among students.

Our vision and dedication drive us to groom students into self-made industrialists of the future.

The Cell focuses on preparing students for aptitude tests, honing their personalities, and fostering industry-institute collaborations.

With a mission to contribute to society through technological innovation, our students consistently demonstrate their value to recruiters, reinforcing the quality of education at MITAOE.

Over 4200 diverse companies span the country.

and M.Tech.

aspirants.

What programs does the MIT Academy of Engineering offer?

The MIT Academy of Engineering provides undergraduate and graduate degrees in a variety of engineering disciplines, including computer engineering, electronics and telecommunications engineering, mechanical engineering, civil engineering, and information technology.

2. Is the MIT Academy of Engineering a private college?

MIT Academic of Engineering is an Empowered Autonomous Engineering college, affiliated with Savitribai Phule Pune University.

Campus Placements Explained: What Students Should Know
Placements aren’t just about landing your first job. For most students, they’re about gaining confidence, financial independence, and a practical start to their career. A good placement gives you early experience, real exposure to workplace expectations, and a smoother shift into the professional world.
The importance of placements lies in helping students apply what they've learnt while giving companies access to fresh talent.
Well-known organisations participate in graduate recruitment to hire engineers, analysts, developers, and management trainees directly from colleges. This process helps them build a pipeline of future-ready professionals and gives students real job opportunities right at the start of their careers.

results.title: Best Private Engineering Colleges in Pune - MITAOE
section_path: Best Private Engineering Colleges in Pune - MITAOE
results.text:
Best Private Engineering Colleges in Pune
Engineering needs lots of learning, understanding, and is considered one of the most challenging fields. Without a results-oriented institution with an expert faculty to shape your enthusiastic mind, achieving what you truly desire after completing a course seems unattainable. Your future is at stake here, and what you choose decides your fate. Unlike well-reputed engineering institutions, the best private engineering colleges in Pune, like MITAOE, make sure that your future is in safe hands. Top in the engineering field in Pune, MITAOE (MIT Academy of Engineering), offers industry-oriented courses that guarantee placement in leading industries.
B.Tech and M.Tech Courses offered for engineering students.
Accredited by NBA (National Board of Accreditation) and NAAC, also honored with the prestige of Academic Autonomy, MIT Academy of Engineering (MITAOE), is one of the best engineering colleges in Pune where students apply every year for admission. From B.tech and M.Tech courses to vibrant campus life and training programs, students here get opportunities for both personal & career growth. The appointment of expert faculty strengthens the quality of education. The advanced B.Tech and M.Tech courses offered by MITAOE help students to be in the lead in this competitive field.
Courses offered in MIT Academy of Engineering (MITAOE)
With courses in fields like Electrical and Electronics Engineering, Civil Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, Computer Science Engineering, IT, and Chemical Engineering, MITAOE attracts individuals with inventive capability, outstanding creativity, and enthusiasm to create solutions that make life easy. With career-oriented courses like M.Tech in Computers science, Mechanical Engineering, and Electronics (VLSI & Embedded Systems), students are equipped with confidence that comes from the progressive curriculum taught by faculties who skilfully impart technical and soft skills expected in the corporate world.
Admission/ examination and fees structure
With the course duration of four years, admission in B.Tech courses generally require 10+2 with 50% marks + MHT CET; fees is ₹1.55 Lakhs for a year. For M.Tech (2 years course) admissions, general criteria are Graduation + GATE, and the fees for a year is ₹1.21 Lakhs. Further, the fee structure varies depending upon the course and duration. The number of Intakes for M.Tech courses is 18 per course. MITAOE guarantees a practical and transparent evaluation system with the online mode of registration for exams and coordination with the parents for a smooth and credible examination system.

results.title: MITAOE | Student Internship Program
results.text:
MITAOE
Student Internship Program
SEMESTER LONG INTERNSHIP PROGRAM
1. To help students gain hands-on professional work experience prior to their graduation.
2. To instill qualities like confidence, maturity, responsibility, and social skills necessary for personal and professional growth.
3. To expose students to state-of-the-art facilities, equipment and professional practices in their respective trades.
4. To expose them to the current technological developments relevant to the subject area of training.
5. To create conditions conducive to quest for knowledge and its applicability on the job.
6. To expose students to the engineer’s responsibilities and ethics.
PROCEDURE
1. CR office conveys various industry requirements / internship opportunities to the students.
2. Students apply for internship work to various industries, appear for the interviews and after their selection they submit the approved “Request Letter for Internship in the Industry” letter to the industry.
3. Students join the industry for internship work and report to faculty mentors every fortnight.
4. After successful completion, internship work is evaluated as per the rubrics given in the guidelines.
ELIGIBILITY
1. No live backlogs are allowed
2. CGPA of 8.50 and above (Up-to 7th Semester)
3. If Recruiter/s (MNCs) have offered semester long internship to the selected student/s (before joining the organization after his / her graduation), then in special case recruited students can apply for the same. (Only criteria-I should be satisfied by the student)
4. Only students satisfying the above criteria can be permitted for semester-long internship in any MNCs / R&D laboratories such as DRDO, NCL, NEERI, CDAC and Institutions like IITs/ NITs / International institutes of repute.
SLIP REPORT (ACADEMIC YEAR 2019-20: JANUARY 2020 – JUNE 2020)
1. During the said period, around 100+ students of final year BTECH students have joined their 16 weeks internship program in various industries.
2. Students engaged in around 28 MNCs for SLIP work.
3. After successful completion, Internship work of all the students get evaluated (Presentation + Internship report) by the panel of three members in every school.
4. Based on their performance & industry evaluation reports, grades are given to the students.
5. Positive impact of SLIP has been observed in students thinking and overall personality.
SLIP REPORT (ACADEMIC YEAR 2020-21: JANUARY 2021 – JUNE 2021)
1. During the said period, 245 students of Final Year BTECH have joined their Semester Long Internship Program in various industries. Out of them, 180 students are doing their semester long internship with stipend.
2. Students engaged in 50 (SMEs+MNCs) industries for their SLIP work.
3. After successful completion, Internship work of all the students get evaluated (Presentation + Internship report) by the panel of three members in every school.
4. Based on their performance & industry evaluation reports, grades are given to the students.
5. Positive impact of SLIP has been observed in students thinking and overall personality.

results.title: MITAOE | Student Internship Program
results.text:
MITAOE
Student Internship Program
OBJECTIVES
1. To provide students possible opportunities to learn, understand and sharpen the real-time technical, managerial and life skills required at the job.
2. To familiarize students to the business environment, which cannot be simulated in the classroom; thus, creating competent professionals for the industry.
3. To help students develop their own job search tools, networking capacities, and interview skills.
4. To expose them to the current technological developments relevant to the subject area of training.
5. To create conditions conducive to quest for knowledge and its applicability on the job.
6. To provide the experience in writing Technical reports/projects.
PROCEDURE
1. CR office conveys various industry requirements / internship opportunities to the students.
2. Students apply for internship work to various industries, appear for the interviews and after their selection they submit the approved “Request Letter for Internship in the Industry” letter to the industry.
3. Students join the industry for internship work and report to faculty mentors every fortnight.
4. After successful completion, internship work is evaluated as per the rubrics given in the guidelines.
SIP REPORT (ACADEMIC YEAR 2019-20 : JUNE-JULY 2019)
1. During the said period, around 1300+ students (SY and TY BTECH students) had successfully completed their 4-8 weeks internship work in various industries.
2. Students engaged in around 350+ industries (SMEs + MNCs) for SIP work.
3. After successful completion, Internship work of all the students get evaluated (Presentation + Internship report) by the panel of three members in every school.
4. Based on their performance & industry evaluation reports, grades are given to the students.
5. Positive impact of SIP has been observed during the placement of the final year BTECH students.
SIP REPORT (ACADEMIC YEAR 2020-21: JUNE 2020 – DECEMBER 2020)
1. During the said period, around 1020+ students (SY and TY BTECH) have completed their 4-8 weeks internship in various industries.
2. Students engaged in 170 (SMEs+MNCs) industries for their SIP work.
3. After successful completion, Internship work of all the students get evaluated (Presentation + Internship report) by the panel of three members in every school.
4. Based on their performance & industry evaluation reports, grades are given to the students.
5. Positive impact of SIP has been observed during the placement of the final year BTECH students.
