Admissions Payments, Refunds and Cancellation PAYMENTS Online Payments using Cards or Net Banking can be done through our partner payment gateway provider, which you will be automatically be redirected to.

In case you face any problems with the payment process, please call us on +91-9071123436 email us at admissions@mitaoe.edu.in.

REFUNDS AND CANCELLATION Refunds, if any, will be credited to the account/card from where the transaction was initiated.

TERMS AND CONDITIONS To avail the services offered at www.mitaoe.ac.in, you must agree to the following terms and conditions.

You are advised to read the term and conditions carefully.

While using any current or future services offered by www.mitaoe.ac.in, whether or not included in the www.mitaoe.ac.in website, you will abide by these Terms, Conditions and Guidelines applicable to such service or business.

PRIVACY POLICY

When you voluntarily send us electronic mail / fillup the form, we will keep a record of this information so that we can respond to you.

We only collect information from you when you register on our site or fill out a form.

Also, when filling out a form on our site, you may be asked to enter your: name, e-mail address or phone number.

You may, however, visit our site anonymously.

In case you have submitted your personal information and contact details, we reserve the rights to Call, SMS, Email or WhatsApp about our products and offers, even if your number has DND activated on it.

Please review our Privacy Policy, which also governs your visit to https://mitaoe.ac.in/Privacy-and-Refund-Policy.php, to fully understand our practices.

ELECTRONIC COMMUNICATION

When you visit www.mitaoe.ac.in or send e-mails to us, you are communicating with us electronically.

By communicating with us, you consent to receive communication from us electronically.

We will communicate with you by e-mail or by posting notices on our website.

You agree that all agreements, notices, disclosures, and other communications that we provide to you electronically, satisfy any legal requirement that such communication be in writing.

PRICES (FEES)

All prices posted on this website are subject to change without notice.

Prices prevailing at commencement of the transaction will apply.

Posted prices for payment excludes convenience fee (customer surcharge) which has to be borne by the end user who makes the payment.

Customer surcharge refers to prices that are included with debit/credit card or net banking charges to be paid by the customer or end user.

In case there are any additional charges or taxes, the same will be mentioned on the website.

PAYMENT Online Payments using debit/credit cards or net banking can be done through our partner payment gateway provider which you will automatically be redirected to respective payment gateway.

TRANSACTION CHARGES FOR ONLINE PAYMENTS ARE AS FOLLOWS Payment Instrument Client Pricing Credit Card 2.30% Debit Card 2.00% UPI 2.00% Net Banking 25 INR Wallets 2.50% CHANGES AND CANCELLATION Changes and Cancellation not allowed.

REFUND Refunds initiated will be credited to the account/card from where the transaction had initiated.

results.text:
18.

Can students do internships abroad?

Yes, students can do internships abroad through exchange programs and international cooperation.

19.

Is an internship paid or unpaid?

It is dependent on the firm providing the internship.

There are both paid and unpaid internship opportunities available.

20.

What is the duration of the internships?

Internships can run anywhere from six to twelve weeks.

21.

What is the student-faculty ratio at the MIT Academy of Engineering?

The student-to-faculty ratio is roughly 15:1, which ensures that students receive customized attention.

22.

What academic resources are available to students?

Students have access to tutoring, mentoring, library resources, and a variety of online learning options, industry resources for webinars and guest lectures.

23.

Are there chances for R&D?

Yes, students are encouraged to participate in research and development activities through the department's labs and centers.

24.

How are students evaluated?

Students are assessed using a combination of continuous assessment, midterm examinations, and end-of-term exams.

25.

What is the academic calendar for this year?

The academic year is normally divided into two semesters, which begin in July and January.

The college's website provides detailed timetables.

26.

What amenities are there on campus?

The campus boasts cutting-edge facilities such as modern classrooms, labs, libraries, dorms, and sports facilities.

27.

Is there accommodation for students?

28.

Are there any medical facilities on campus?

Yes, there is a medical center on campus where students can see a certified doctor.

29.

What eating options exist on campus?

30.

Are there any recreational facilities on campus?

31.

What extracurricular activities are available?

Students can engage in a variety of extracurricular activities, such as sports, cultural events, technical groups, and student organizations.

Students have 28 clubs at college to choose the extra curricular activity from for enhancement of their personality.

32.

Do students have the opportunity to participate in national and international competitions?

Yes, students are encouraged to compete in numerous national and international events, and they have had great success.

33.

What student groups are available?

Students can participate in technical, cultural, and social activities through 28 various clubs and organizations.

34.

How can students participate in campus activities?

Students can join clubs and groups, attend events, and volunteer for numerous activities through the student affairs office.

35.

Are there any leadership development programs for students?

Yes, the institution provides leadership development programs to help students develop their talents and prepare for the future.

36.

What is the fee structure for undergraduate programs?

To know the fee structure of the UG courses of MITAOE, please fill the application form and you will know the details.

Fee Structure 37.

Are scholarships accessible to students?

Yes, several scholarships are offered based on merit, financial need, and other criteria.

Please contact our Admissions team for more details.

Contact Now 38.

How should students apply for scholarships?

Fill the admission form, contact our admission team or meet the student section for more details on this.

Contact Now 39.

Are there financial options available to students?

Yes, the college has agreements with various banks to give education loans to students.

40.

How can I pay my school fees?

Tuition payments can be paid electronically via the college portal or contact our admission team or meet the student section for more details on this.

Contact Now 41.

The School of Mechanical Engineering's Board of Studies was introduced for curriculum development from 2016. It is the driving factor behind our academic success. This multidisciplinary committee, comprised of prominent academic members, industry experts, and student representatives, ensures that our programs remain at the forefront of innovation and market relevance. They work together to create leading-edge courses, assess academic performance, and direct research. We constantly adapt to the ever-changing landscape of Mechanical engineering thanks to their dedication, ensuring our students obtain a top-tier education that meets the demands of today's world. Join us in congratulating them on their dedication to academic achievement and the success of our students and alumni.
MOU
A Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) is a formal agreement between a college or university and a company is a formal agreement that outlines the intentions and objectives of the collaboration between the two entities. The MOU serves as a framework for their partnership and helps establish a clear understanding of their respective roles and responsibilities. The specific objectives of an MOU can vary based on the nature of the partnership, but some common objectives includes Educational Collaboration, Research and Development, Skill Development and Training, Industry-Academia Interface, Recruitment and Talent Pipeline, Exchange Programs and Intellectual Property Rights.

results.title: Meet the Minds Behind the Learning: Understanding MIT AOE’s Faculty Directory
section_path: Meet the Minds Behind the Learning: Understanding MIT AOE’s Faculty Directory
results.text:
Meet the Minds Behind the Learning: Understanding MIT AOE’s Faculty Directory
What Is the Faculty Directory?

The faculty directory at MIT AOE gives students a clear view of the academic framework across departments.

It highlights who leads each department.

This can be helpful when students need guidance, recommendations, or departmental approvals.

Each listing is department-specific and shows the name and designation of the Head of Department (HOD).

These pages also offer a general overview of the department's focus, the types of programmes offered, and, sometimes, the facilities available within each academic unit.

In this format, students get a reliable view of the leadership structure — even if complete individual faculty profiles are not shown for every teaching staff member.

How Can Students Access Faculty Profiles?
MIT AOE does not currently provide detailed public profiles for all teaching staff.

Nevertheless, information about departments and their heads is accessible through the official website.

Here’s how students can find it:
Visit www.mitaoe.ac.in
Select the Academics menu from the main navigation bar
Click on Academic Departments
Choose your department of interest
The page will display the Head of Department, along with department highlights and basic contact details
While this isn’t a comprehensive directory of all academic members, it does provide students with a verified way to identify who leads each department and where to direct subject-related questions.

What Information Is Available in the Directory?

The content you’ll find in the directory is brief but specific.

It typically includes:
Information Type
What You’ll See
Name
Full name of the Head of Department
Designation
Example: Professor, Associate Professor
Department
Assigned academic division
Contact Information
Email ID or college phone extension (if listed)

In some departments, a short write-up explains the department’s goals, academic strengths, and focus areas.

For those looking to understand who leads a particular subject stream, this information is helpful.

Why Is the Faculty Directory Important for Students?

At MIT AOE, the faculty structure plays a key role in how students access academic support, obtain approvals, and plan further studies.

Here’s why the faculty directory matters:
Follow these steps to apply for MIT AOE:
Quick access to academic contacts
Students often need help outside lecture hours — for lab queries, mini projects, or event planning.

Knowing who heads the department helps them reach out without confusion.

Clear department-level leadership
The directory lists the Head of Department (HOD) for each academic area.

This gives students a clear contact for subject or course-related matters.

Support with permissions and guidance
For approvals, project topics, or clarification on academic policies, students can directly approach their HOD through official channels.

Entry point for further academic interaction
While individual professors and lecturers aren’t listed publicly, the HOD can guide students to the right faculty members within the department.

Useful for research-focused students
Those looking to engage with research faculty or planning for higher studies can start by identifying which departments align with their interests.

Structured and reliableThe directory provides a verified, formal way to navigate the academic system.

Even without complete profiles, it supports student queries straightforwardly.

Closing Note
The university educators at MIT AOE form the backbone of its academic structure.

While full faculty listings aren’t published online, the department-level details help students find the right point of contact.

It’s a simple yet reliable system that supports clear academic communication.

Frequently Asked Questions
Getting ready to apply?

results.title: Admissions at MIT Academy of Engineering
section_path: Admissions at MIT Academy of Engineering
results.text:
Admissions at MIT Academy of Engineering
Admissions at MIT Academy of Engineering (MIT AOE)
Choosing where to study engineering can feel like a big decision.

While reputation and location do matter, most students also want a college that is fair and easy to apply to.

For many across Maharashtra and other states, MIT Academy of Engineering (MIT AOE) in Pune fits that picture.

It’s recognised not just for academics but for how clearly it handles admissions.

Each year, thousands look to secure a seat at MIT AOE.

To do that, you need to understand the full system.

Knowing what to prepare, when to act, and which forms to fill can make all the difference.

The institutions admission process here follows Maharashtra’s centralised rules.

If you are applying from within the state or outside it, the steps are the same.

This guide walks you through what you’ll need, from documents to entrance tests.

What is the Admission Process?

Admissions to MIT AOE are done through the state-run Centralised Admission Process (CAP) conducted by the Maharashtra State Common Entrance Test Cell.

This ensures all students are considered on the same grounds.

Here’s how the enrolment process works:
Step
Activity
Mode
1
Register on CET Cell Portal
Online
2
Upload required documents
Online
3
Fill out preferences for colleges & branches
Online
4
Document verification
E-Scrutiny
5
Merit list declared
Online
6
Provisional allotment
Online
7
Accept seat & pay admission fees
Online
8
Report to the allotted institute
In-person
Allotment is strictly merit-based admission, and seats are offered based on ranking, preferences, and category rules.

What Are the Eligibility Criteria for Admission?
Minimum eligibility includes:
Should have passed their 10+2 level studies or equivalent, ensuring both Physics and Mathematics were part of the core curriculum.

One additional subject from Chemistry / Biology / Biotechnology / Technical Vocational.

At least 45% marks for the General category (40% for reserved categories) in aggregate.

Must be an Indian national.

Candidates applying under the All India quota must meet the same conditions, and their scores will be considered separately under the relevant quota.

Which Entrance Exams Are Accepted for Admission?

To get into engineering in Maharashtra, most students sit the MHT-CET — short for Maharashtra Health and Technical Common Entrance Test.

This is the main exam accepted for first-year engineering courses at MIT AOE.

There are two exam routes you can use, depending on the quota you’re applying under:
MHT-CET – required for students applying through the Maharashtra State quota or Open Category
JEE Main – accepted for those applying under the All India quota or through institute-level seats
Your exam score is an important part of the process.

When you fill out your application, you’ll need to enter your marks from either MHT-CET or JEE Main.

Without one of these, you won’t receive a rank in the CAP rounds, which means you won’t be considered for a seat.

How Can Students Apply for Admission to MIT AOE?

The application procedure is fully online and follows a fixed timeline released by the CET Cell.

During the CAP rounds, MIT AOE does not accept direct applications from students — everything must go through the official portal.

Follow these steps to apply for MIT AOE:

results.title: All About MHT-CET 2021
results.text:
All About MHT-CET 2021
The Maharashtra Common Entrance Test is an annual online examination for admission to several Undergraduate programs and Post Graduate in Maharashtra's state colleges. MIT Academy of Engineering is one of the 401 colleges that accept MHT-CET scores for admission. Because MIT AOE is one of the best engineering colleges in Pune, it receives a large number of applications each year. Those interested in enrolling for any of the courses at the institute should first apply for the MHT-CET exam and then apply for admission to MITAOE, Alandi as soon as possible.
The MHT CET 2021 eligibility has been released along with the application form. Applicants for MHT CET 2021 must ensure that they meet the admission standards; else, their application will be rejected. MHT CET 2021 eligibility conditions can be found here. Candidates who have passed/appearing in the qualifying examination, i.e. HSC/12th Standard or its equivalent, and who are of Indian nationality are eligible to apply for MHT CET 2021. There are no age limitations for MHT-CET 2021 admission.

results.title: Admissions at MIT Academy of Engineering
section_path: Admissions at MIT Academy of Engineering
results.text:
Admissions at MIT Academy of Engineering
Admissions at MIT Academy of Engineering (MIT AOE)
Choosing where to study engineering can feel like a big decision.

While reputation and location do matter, most students also want a college that is fair and easy to apply to.

For many across Maharashtra and other states, MIT Academy of Engineering (MIT AOE) in Pune fits that picture.

It’s recognised not just for academics but for how clearly it handles admissions.

Each year, thousands look to secure a seat at MIT AOE.

To do that, you need to understand the full system.

Knowing what to prepare, when to act, and which forms to fill can make all the difference.

The institutions admission process here follows Maharashtra’s centralised rules.

If you are applying from within the state or outside it, the steps are the same.

This guide walks you through what you’ll need, from documents to entrance tests.

What is the Admission Process?

Admissions to MIT AOE are done through the state-run Centralised Admission Process (CAP) conducted by the Maharashtra State Common Entrance Test Cell.

This ensures all students are considered on the same grounds.

Here’s how the enrolment process works:
Step
Activity
Mode
1
Register on CET Cell Portal
Online
2
Upload required documents
Online
3
Fill out preferences for colleges & branches
Online
4
Document verification
E-Scrutiny
5
Merit list declared
Online
6
Provisional allotment
Online
7
Accept seat & pay admission fees
Online
8
Report to the allotted institute
In-person
Allotment is strictly merit-based admission, and seats are offered based on ranking, preferences, and category rules.

What Are the Eligibility Criteria for Admission?
Minimum eligibility includes:
Should have passed their 10+2 level studies or equivalent, ensuring both Physics and Mathematics were part of the core curriculum.

One additional subject from Chemistry / Biology / Biotechnology / Technical Vocational.

At least 45% marks for the General category (40% for reserved categories) in aggregate.

Must be an Indian national.

Candidates applying under the All India quota must meet the same conditions, and their scores will be considered separately under the relevant quota.

Which Entrance Exams Are Accepted for Admission?

To get into engineering in Maharashtra, most students sit the MHT-CET — short for Maharashtra Health and Technical Common Entrance Test.

This is the main exam accepted for first-year engineering courses at MIT AOE.

There are two exam routes you can use, depending on the quota you’re applying under:
MHT-CET – required for students applying through the Maharashtra State quota or Open Category
JEE Main – accepted for those applying under the All India quota or through institute-level seats
Your exam score is an important part of the process.

When you fill out your application, you’ll need to enter your marks from either MHT-CET or JEE Main.

Without one of these, you won’t receive a rank in the CAP rounds, which means you won’t be considered for a seat.

How Can Students Apply for Admission to MIT AOE?

The application procedure is fully online and follows a fixed timeline released by the CET Cell.

During the CAP rounds, MIT AOE does not accept direct applications from students — everything must go through the official portal.

Follow these steps to apply for MIT AOE:

section_path: Best BTech Colleges in Pune, India
results.text:
Best BTech Colleges in Pune, India
Best B.Tech Engineering Courses
India, with its rich educational heritage, stands at the forefront of higher education globally. The importance of higher education in India cannot be overstated; it's the foundation upon which the nation builds its intellectual and industrial might. Central to this edifice are the BTech colleges, which play a pivotal role in shaping careers, and molding the next generation of innovators, thinkers, and leaders. What's exciting is the diversity of BTech specializations available. As one of the best BTech colleges in India, MIT Academy of Engineering offers a plethora of courses, ensuring every student finds their calling. Top BTech Specializations in India
Computer Science and Engineering
Mechanical Engineering
Electrical Engineering
Civil Engineering
Electronics and Communication Engineering
Chemical Engineering
Aerospace Engineering
Data Science and Artificial Intelligence
Join us at MIT Academy of Engineering and carve out a niche for yourself in the engineering world. Factors to Consider When Choosing a BTech College
When it comes to engineering education, the choice of institution is paramount. Here's what you should keep in mind:
- Accreditation and Recognition
At MITAOE, we pride ourselves on our Academic Autonomous status. Further strengthening our credibility are recognitions such as the NAAC certificate of accreditation, ISO certification, a commendable NIRF institute ranking, and the esteemed acknowledgment by UGThis makes us stand out among the best colleges for BTech. - Infrastructure and facilities Our sprawling campus is a hub of amenities - from a well-stocked library, buzzing cafeteria, seamless Wi-Fi connectivity, and comfortable hostels to state-of-the-art computer labs, dedicated sports areas, a vibrant ed-cell, an on-site medical hospital, and much more, catering to every need of our students.
- Faculty expertise and qualifications
Our faculty members are the backbone of MITAOE. With vast industry experience and academic qualifications, they mold the leaders of tomorrow.
- Placement opportunities and industry connections
Our Career Advisory Services plays a pivotal role in guiding students with career services, soft skill development programs, and preparatory courses. With a growing relationship with both Indian and international firms and universities, we've had top recruiters like Accenture, Capgemini, Cisco, HCL, JP Morgan, Mahindra, Tata, and more choosing our students year after year. - Alumni network and success stories Our alumni have etched success stories worldwide. Their achievements speak volumes about our education quality and the invaluable network they offer to current students. Admission Process for BTech Colleges
- Entrance exams
Admission to MITAOE is based on scores from JEE Main or MHT-CET Entrance Exams. Additionally, we offer Direct Entry to the 2nd year of the B.Tech. program for candidates holding a Diploma in Engineering & Technology or a B.SDegree, provided they have achieved a minimum of 45% marks (40% for reserved category) in aggregate. - Eligibility criteria
Prospective students should have cleared their HSC Examination with a minimum of 50% marks. We also offer a 5% mark relaxation for the reserved category.
- Counseling and seat allocation procedures
At MITAOE, post results, we invite selected candidates for a structured counseling session. Our transparent seat allocation process ensures that deserving candidates secure their preferred specialization. Campus Life and Extracurricular Activities
- Student clubs and organizations
From the expressive AALEKH - Art Elated Club to the analytical CodeChef Campus Chapter, from the spirited Menace Dance group to the innovative Robotics team, there’s a club for every passion. Whether it's the artistry in Shutterbugs or the tranquility in Yoga and Meditation, MITAOE has it all.
- Technical and cultural fests
TEDxMITAOE is our crowning jewel, encapsulating the essence of ‘Ideas Worth Spreading’. Under the theme "SONIC BOOM", we deliver inspiring talks, stirring demonstrations, and invigorating performances, ensuring an enriching experience.
- Sports facilities and achievements We champion both mind and body. From indoor games like carom and chess to inter-collegiate football, our athletes have clinched gold in swimming, kabaddi, and more, making us one of the B Tech colleges in Pune with a holistic approach. Industry Interface and Internship Programs
In the world of B Tech engineering courses, theoretical knowledge is vital, but practical exposure is paramount. With tie-ups with giants like ONGC, Accenture, and Tata, we facilitate internships and projects, ensuring hands-on experience. With an average salary of 8 lacs and a highest of 18 lacs, 60% of our batch shines bright in the industry. Our holistic approach ensures not just knowledge but skill development, and skyrocketing employability.

results.title: Top Civil Engineering College in Pune
section_path: Top Civil Engineering College in Pune
results.text:
Top Civil Engineering College in Pune
It is overwhelming to decide on the Civil Engineering courses in Pune since the city is full of colleges. There is no need for you to fret since we have got your back, we will tell you about the course and the best engineering institute in Pune from where you can pursue it as well.
About Civil Engineering Courses
Civil Engineering is one of the most popular courses; it allows the students to learn to maintain, design, and construct buildings, roads, and other related things. Not from today, but since the past many years everyone requires a civil engineer to get the perfect construction done. Thus, it is vital that students have information about various Civil Engineering courses.
Civil Engineering Course Details
Under any one of the Civil Engineering courses in Pune, the students learn to maintain, build, and design buildings, bridges, roads, natural formations, and many other things. Any student from a science (mathematics & physics compulsory) background in +2 can pursue the course.
Different Courses of Civil Engineering
Bachelors in Technology or Bachelors in Engineering
The students can pursue a Bachelor's degree after completing 10+2.
For obtaining admissions in the top engineering colleges, the student needs to appear for the JEE Mains and Advanced. A good rank in JEE Mains helps secure a seat in the best colleges, but, to appear for the JEE Mains, the students must score above 75% in their 10+2 boards exam with the subjects physics, chemistry, and mathematics. The students require a minimum of 50% marks (PCM) in 10+2 to become eligible for average colleges.
Masters in Technology or Masters in Engineering
The students can pursue a Master's degree after completing a Bachelor's degree in the same field.
The eligibility criteria can vary from one college to another. Many colleges do not take admissions until the GATE score of a student is 60%. Whereas, if the student did their Bachelor’s from that particular university only, the student will not need to appear for GATE, they can get admission on the basis of their bachelor's CPI.
Master of Philosophy or Doctor of Philosophy
The students can apply for a Doctorate after completing their post-graduation or Masters.
The college from which a candidate can pursue a doctorate depends on their GATE or NET score. Those with high scores, get into the top colleges.
Academic Degree or Diploma Courses
A student can pursue a diploma course after completing 10+2 with PCM background.
There are plenty of Civil Engineering diploma courses that a student can pursue. It is a course of 3 years, mostly requiring the candidates to have at least 40% marks in their 10+2 boards exam.
Distance Education
Students can pursue distance education in Civil Engineering after clearing 10+2 with the PCM stream.
Many universities of Pune provide distance learning programs to help students all across pursue subjects of their choice. It helps the students overcome any hindrance they may be facing in pursuing regular classes.
For anyone who lives in Pune, the best option to pursue Civil Engineering should not be a question since MIT Academy of Engineering (MITAOE), Pune, has a solid reputation for providing the students with the best faculty and campus life. It has a Grade 'A' from NAAC and is the best spot for an undergraduate student to shape his/her future in the field of Civil Engineering. The academy offers seven full-time B.Tech. programs in the fields of Chemical, Computer, Civil, Electronics, IT, Mechanical, and ENTC Engineering.

results.title: All About MHT-CET 2021
results.text:
All About MHT-CET 2021
The Maharashtra Common Entrance Test is an annual online examination for admission to several Undergraduate programs and Post Graduate in Maharashtra's state colleges. MIT Academy of Engineering is one of the 401 colleges that accept MHT-CET scores for admission. Because MIT AOE is one of the best engineering colleges in Pune, it receives a large number of applications each year. Those interested in enrolling for any of the courses at the institute should first apply for the MHT-CET exam and then apply for admission to MITAOE, Alandi as soon as possible.
The MHT CET 2021 eligibility has been released along with the application form. Applicants for MHT CET 2021 must ensure that they meet the admission standards; else, their application will be rejected. MHT CET 2021 eligibility conditions can be found here. Candidates who have passed/appearing in the qualifying examination, i.e. HSC/12th Standard or its equivalent, and who are of Indian nationality are eligible to apply for MHT CET 2021. There are no age limitations for MHT-CET 2021 admission.

results.title: M.Tech in Computer Engineering – MITAOE
results.text:
M.Tech in Computer Engineering – MITAOE
Lakhs of students sit for entrance exams every year to get into an esteemed engineering college. Engineering is one of the preferred courses as students get exposed to a world full of opportunities. Students can either take up a lucrative job or can go for higher studies like M.Tech in Computer Engineering or any preferred field. If a student wishes to pursue higher studies, it is best to opt for top engineering colleges in Pune, offering good technical education and placement guidance.
About M.Tech in Computer Engineering at MITAOE
MITAOE offers three PG courses, one of them being M.Tech in Computer Engineering. The two-year course helps the students to get in-depth knowledge in a Computer Science specialization. The entire curriculum is divided into six semesters, four semesters of theoretical and practical exams, while the last two semesters are allotted for project work.
The master's program makes the student shine in the competitive world and contributes more to the technological field.
Admission process for M.Tech
The number of intake for M.Tech Computer Engineering is 18. Every year, the admission procedures, courses, and forms are published by the Directorate of Technical Education. Candidates must fill-up the admission form and submit it before the deadline to get into a college.
To get into the M.Tech course, the student must have obtained a good score from the GATE exam with at least 50% in B.Tech. Other criteria are announced by the officials every year. All the rules and fee structure is declared every year by the Maharashtra government.
R&D department
MITAOE has an excellent research cell to help students with a platform to showcase their ideas. Students and faculty can use the R&D department to develop new technology. The R&D department was developed in 2008 to help the students work and explore the domain of Computer Engineering. The department helps the students channel their intellectual thoughts and energy to focus on researching new ideas and develop innovations.
Student council and clubs
Apart from a good curriculum, MITAOE also has a number of educational clubs, and the student committee also helps the students to grow more. Clubs like robotics and Hacker rank helps the students to keep learning and shaping their creative and intellectual ideas into reality.
Classroom programs, along with these educational clubs, help the students to love and understand the subject better.
Placement opportunities
MITAOE has a placement cell that assists and guides the students towards their dream career path. The college has tied-up with some international colleges so that interested students can choose to study abroad. The college also has an E-cell (entrepreneurial and development), which helps budding entrepreneurs realize their dreams of successfully creating a startup. The college has tie-ups with many industries and companies and helps the students in landing their dream job.

Overview of Profile:
Dr. Swapnil Patare currently serves as an Assistant Professor within the School of Civil Engineering, demonstrating a strong inclination towards both research and innovative teaching. He earned his Ph.D. degree from IIT Bombay, complemented by a Master's degree in Structural Engineering and a Bachelor's degree in Civil Engineering from SPPU Pune. With over a decade of teaching experience and an additional year in the industrial sector, Dr. Patare possesses a wealth of pedagogical expertise.
His research expertise encompasses the intricate domain of structural analysis, focusing on the behavior of slender elements subjected to bending, buckling, and vibration. Dr. Patare also explores the integration of smart materials with structural components to effectively control deflection and vibration. Proficient in computational tools such as Python and LaTeX, he has honed his skills over the course of his academic journey.
With 12 years of academic experience, Dr. Patare has imparted knowledge in subjects including structural analysis, design, and construction management. His commitment to innovation extends to the application of Artificial Intelligence and Data Science in the realms of structural engineering and construction management, highlighting his dedication to pushing the boundaries of the field.

results.title: Top BTech Colleges in Pune | MIT AOE
results.text:
Top BTech Colleges in Pune
A Bachelor of Technology (BTech) is an undergraduate academic degree awarded to students who have completed a four-year study program at one of the university's associated schools. BTech is regarded as a skill-oriented program and the most popular course among science-based students. Hence, students must choose the best BTech colleges in Pune for their bright future.
Many prominent and blue-chip firms provide positions to students who have completed a BTech from a reputable college with specializations in various subjects, including mechanical, civil, computer science, information technology, electrical, electronic, and communication engineering. The curriculum helps students polish their abilities and boost their future prospects depending on their area of interest.
After completing a BTech, there are several employment options in technical and management domains. Any engineering graduate in India or overseas can expect to land a public or private sector job. Also, students taking part in the placement drive should update their resumes and diligently prepare for the interview so reputable companies can hire them. BTech graduates are hired as researchers, consultants, and subject matter experts in addition to becoming engineers.
Enroll Yourself in one of the top BTech Colleges in Pune
If you search online for the top BTech colleges in Pune, you will get several answers, but among these, MITAOE (MIT Academy of Engineering) has proven to be the best.
MIT Academy of Engineering (MITAOE), established in 1999, is located in Alandi, Pune. It was founded under MAEER (Maharashtra Academy of Engineering Education and Research) by the respected Prof. Dr Vishwanath D. Karad, executive director and founder of the MIT group. Here are some reasons that make MITAOE as one of the most renowned BTech colleges in Pune.
Exceptional Academic Record
MITAOE offers seven undergraduate programs (B.Tech) and three post-graduate programs (M.Tech). The Savitribai Phule Pune University (SPPU) has affiliated MIT institute with the 'Best Engineering college' award. The institute has established itself as a 'Centre of Excellence in engineering education and research by excelling in academics and research. The UGC has awarded the institute 'Academic Autonomy, ' recognizing the institute's high-quality educational techniques, research, faculty, and state-of-the-art facilities that satisfy international standards. The NBA accreditation of all branches, the NAAC 'A' grade, and the UGC's 2(F) and 12(B) attest to our commitment to quality.
Best Placements
MITAOE works hard to instill practical and professional skills in students while also providing them with excellent job possibilities. It has partnerships with firms such as Amazon, Mahindra, Accenture, Spicejet, IBM, and JP Morgan to provide industrial tours, soft-skills training workshops, technical contests, guest lecturers, etc., hi-tech live projects, and expert assistance on add-on courses. Prospective students can expect a strong return on investment because of the institute's outstanding placement records.

All in all, MITAOE, the top engineering college in Pune, fulfills its mission to provide students with the best educational and learning experience that will help take their careers to new heights.

results.title: Best BTech Colleges in India | MIT AOE
results.text:
Best BTech Colleges in India
The Bachelor of Technology (B. Tech) is a four-year undergraduate programme divided into eight semesters. It is a professional engineering degree that is available in various fields, including Computer Science, Electronics and Communication, Information Technology, Electrical, Civil, and Mechanical Engineering.
The benefit of a B.TEch is that being a professional degree opens up many employment options in technical and management disciplines in both the public and commercial sectors in India and overseas. You might potentially be hired as a researcher, consultant, or other types of professional.
Various national, state and institute-level engineering entrance tests are held for admissions to B.Tech programmes to get into a top engineering college. One of the top Engineering colleges in Pune is MITAOE. Let us understand what makes it a distinct institute.
What Makes MITAOE One Of The Best B.Tech Colleges in India?
Here are some of the prominent reasons it attracts many students from all across the country.
Undergraduate Research Opportunities Program
As a freshman at MITAOE, you may work on cutting-edge research alongside some brightest minds. You'll be able to engage in a UROP, or Undergraduate Research Opportunities Program, at MITAOE. Professors at other colleges frequently accept only graduate students to assist them with their research.
It's also not difficult; 85 percent of students graduate with a UROP. An extra benefit is that you can get compensated for your efforts. That's a great deal. This makes MITAOE one of the best B.Tech colleges in India.
Resources
When you attend MITAOE, you will access the university's supplementary resources. They include free admission to the Museum, which has exhibitions, an Idea Hub, workshops, concerts, and more regarding STEAM issues and is situated on campus (Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts, Maths). Students and instructors often lead demonstrations.
Students can also get cheap tickets to the Museum of Fine Arts and the Boston Ballet, among other arts and cultural organizations. There are various choices for students who want to go about town, including campus shuttle services.
Placements
There is a 6 lakh package that is easily provided for B.tech civil engineer, structural engineer NSO on they offer many more degrees at the college such as civil, CSE, etc., mech and many more. Placements are the most important aspects and the kick-start to your career journey. Great, desirable packages make MITAOE one of the best B.Tech colleges in India.
Highly Competent Faculty
This college's professors are highly competent, with nearly every faculty member holding a PhD. They're all really helpful, and they're all very competent in their industries. The course curriculum is flexible and incorporates a lot of practical work and academics, giving students a thorough grasp of the topic and preparing them for the workplace.
As an autonomous institution, the curriculum is constantly revised to meet industry demands, and the placement ratio is good compared to other colleges.
Infrastructure
When you have to spend four years in an institution, infrastructure plays an important role. The hostel facilities in the institute are quite decent, and each room has its washroom. The Institute has a common area that is so clean and is used by students for group studies and parties. Institute also offer good-quality food to the students, which is rare in many institutes around the country. It provides students with a safe, healthy, and homely environment to thrive.
Closing
If you plan to get admission into an engineering college, MITAOE is the best choice for you. The one of a kind faculty, infrastructure, facilities and quality of education makes it the most desired private engineering college in India. The very affordable fee structure and placements are the cherries on the top.

results.title: Best Engineering Colleges in Pune for BTech | MIT AOE
section_path: Best Engineering Colleges in Pune for BTech
results.text:
Best Engineering Colleges in Pune for BTech
Choosing a good college is tough as several engineering colleges offer different courses. A student willing to get admission to top engineering colleges in Pune encounters the same problem. In Pune, there are a total of 129 engineering colleges. Out of these 129 colleges, nine are public or government, and 120 are private colleges. Choosing the right college out of these many colleges is a tough job. Among the best engineering colleges in Pune for BTech is MIT AOE.
Why choose BTech?
BTech is one of the most favored courses by science students with diverse fields such as Civil, Mechanical, Computer Science, Electrical, Information Technology, Electronics and Communication Engineering. Depending on your area of interest, the course offers candidates to hone their skills. No matter which area you pick, you are able to explore an ample amount of job opportunities after each technical programme, which gives you a promising profession.
The career options after BTech are much higher in managerial and technical fields. BTech graduates are also recruited as consultants, researchers, and subject matter experts. Any engineering graduate may hope to get a good job in the public or private sector abroad or in India itself.
Who can apply for BTech?
The basic eligibility standard for BTech is 12th standard with Physics, Chemistry and Mathematics. However, there are more criteria in every entrance exam and institute. Some of the institutes also give admission to their courses on a merit basis, i.e., based on class 12 board examination marks. For admissions, the most typical BTech entrance examinations are JEE Main and JEE Advanced. Students may need to appear for many state and private level entrance examinations to get into BTech courses at a college of their liking.
Best Engineering College in Pune for BTech - MIT AOE
MIT AOE (MIT Academy of Engineering), Alandi, Pune, was founded in 1999. The college offers seven different UG programs (B.Tech) and three PG programs (M.Tech). The programs are offered across significant engineering domains such as Civil, Mechanical, Chemical, Computer, Information Technology, Electronics and Telecommunication and Electronics.
Why go for BTech at MIT AOE?
MIT AOE is awarded ‘academic autonomy by the UGC (University Grants Commission). It is one of the top engineering colleges in Pune, with an ‘A’ grade accreditation from NAAC for all courses. There are many benefits of academic autonomy for all stakeholders of the institute.
Updated and contemporary syllabi; flexibility in revising as per industry needs.
Innovative strategies for teaching and learning; skill-based teaching.
Interdisciplinary practices in academics and research.
An extensive selection of subjects
Opportunities for collaborations with foreign & national universities and various companies.
Better placement options.
Support for higher education in India & abroad.
Internships and scholarships for projects.
Guest lectures and training programs by eminent experts from academia and industry.
Final Words
BTech offers much scope in today’s world. That’s why many high school students work towards getting into an engineering college after the 12th standard. The best way to be successful in this field, however, lies in the college you choose. With years of experience, excellent faculty, and a holistic syllabus, MIT AOE offers the platform you need to reach your career goals as an engineer. So, start your journey with one of the best engineering colleges in Pune for BTech - MIT AOE today.

results.title: Infrastructure and Facilities at MIT AOE: A Practical Overview for Students
section_path: Infrastructure and Facilities at MIT AOE: A Practical Overview for Students
results.text:
Infrastructure and Facilities at MIT AOE: A Practical Overview for Students
Infrastructure and Facilities at MIT AOE
When you first walk into the MIT Academy of Engineering, what you notice is not at all just the buildings.

It is the way everything feels ready for students.

For example, there are spaces where people hang around before class, and labs that smell faintly of machines and new wires.

Then there are quiet corners where you can catch up on notes.

Infrastructure and facilities shape how easy it is actually to get things done while you are here.

MIT AOE’s campus isn’t massive, but it’s well thought out.

Each building seems to have a clear job, so you’re not wandering for ages trying to find a lab or a classroom.

There’s usually a buzz between lectures, with students moving quickly from one block to another.

Even the walkways are planned so you do not waste time during busy days.

What infrastructure and facilities are available on campus?

Students here have access to proper campus facilities.

This covers everything from the classrooms you sit in every day to the labs you visit for practicals, the library, and a few simple places to grab food or study in groups.

There’s a dedicated building for each department, so it’s easy to find your teachers or lab supervisors.

The central library is bigger than you’d expect and stays pretty busy, especially before exams.

Labs are updated with what each department needs — you’ll see all the standard equipment, and nothing is outdated.

There are computer rooms with internet.

Hostels are available (though spots fill up quickly).

If you want a break, there’s a cafeteria and a few shaded areas outside that are good for quick chats or revision.

MIT AOE has also worked to keep things clean and safe.

You’ll find staff around most days making sure the equipment is working and the common areas are tidy.

How do facilities support student learning?

The academic infrastructure does what it should.

For example, classrooms are simple but have projectors and whiteboards.

And labs are scheduled so you actually get time to use the gear.

Group study is a real thing here — the library has dedicated spots for both solo learning and team work.

Teachers actively use technology in classrooms, going beyond traditional chalk-and-talk methods.

Assignments and projects become easier to manage with ready access to computers and printing facilities.

Student amenities like sports spaces and standard rooms give you a reason to stay back on campus instead of heading straight home after classes.

When the pressure ramps up, having good spaces makes it less stressful to finish significant assignments or prepare for finals.

Are research facilities included in the campus infrastructure?

Yes, research facilities are part of the setup.

Postgrads and final-year students use dedicated labs and project rooms for their research work.

Some departments have their own spaces with specialised equipment.

There is also access to online journals and a few technical publications in the library for anyone doing a project or looking to publish.

Why facilities matter at MIT AOE
At MIT AOE, attention to details means you do not have to scramble for basic things.

You get your lab time, you find the right teacher, and you’ve got a dedicated spot to study.

Modern classrooms and practical labs aren’t a luxury; they’re part of ensuring students actually learn what they’re supposed to.

When facilities work, students waste less time and can focus on the parts of uni that matter most.

Wrapping Up
Infrastructure at MIT AOE is smart and student-friendly.

results.text:
Best Autonomous Engineering College in Pune
MIT Academy of Engineering
The MIT Academy of Engineering (MIT AOE) is a well-known autonomous engineering college in the Alandi neighborhood of Pune. The institute, which was founded in 1999, has emerged as a pioneer in the area of engineering education and research. MIT AOE has consistently been ranked among the top engineering colleges in Pune and India, with a strong emphasis on innovation, industry collaborations, and interdisciplinary learning.
In this blog, we are going to discuss what makes MIT AOE an excellent college of engineering.
Affiliation and Accreditations
MIT AOE is an autonomous college affiliated with SavitriBai Phule University (SPPU).
The institute is approved by the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE).
It has also got approval from the Government of Maharashtra.
The institute is accredited by NAAC with an ‘A’ grade for all programs.
In addition, the institute should also have the 2(F) & 12(B) status of the UGC.
The institute has made a name for itself through academic and research excellence, and it continues to expand as a 'Centre of Excellence' in engineering education and research.
It has ranked 1st in Maharashtra & 11th in India amongst the finest Private Engineering Colleges by Outlook Survey.
Why is MIT AOE the Best Autonomous Engineering College in Pune?
There are many reasons why MIT AOE is considered the best autonomous college in Pune. Let us elaborate on some of them:
Industry Oriented Curriculum
The curriculum at MIT AOE is designed to provide students with a well-rounded education that includes theoretical knowledge as well as practical experience. Mechanical Engineering, Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering, Computer Engineering, Chemical Technology, and Civil Engineering are among the undergraduate programs available at the college. The courses are updated regularly to reflect the most recent industry trends and technologies.

results.title: Sports
results.text:
MIT AOE offers inter-college competitions, wellness workshops and fitness challenges, where the value of sport in schools is communicated as an integral part of student life.

Participation in sport is a way to gain academic and athletic experience as students learn to manage and sustain difficult engineering coursework through their participation in fitness.

Faculty mentors guide students, where they learn teamwork, strategic planning, and leadership.

Instituting sports into the daily delivery of MIT AOE, a private college in Pune for engineering students, can ensure that they lead balanced lives that facilitate physical well-being, integrated with mental toughness and social skills.

It transforms students into health-focused professionals who also have the grit necessary to be excited to engage in challenging experiences.

Sports at MIT AOE are not merely an extra-curricular activity, but rather an essential aspect of campus life where fitness, education, and self-enhancement are intertwined.

How is Research Conducted?

( MIT AOE )
Research at MIT Academy of Engineering (MIT AOE) is done in a systematic and experiential manner by integrating theoretical knowledge with practical exposure.

Students work on varied projects across streams such as Mechanical, Computer, Chemical, and Electronics Engineering with experienced faculty mentors.

The research methods at MIT AOE focus on problem identification, literature study, experimentation, data analysis, and documentation to produce rigorous results.

Sophisticated laboratories, fully equipped workshops, and collaborative areas facilitate cutting-edge research techniques, such as simulation, prototyping, and applied experimentation.

MIT AOE promotes scholarly research that complements industry requirements, developing internships, live projects, and research publications.

The integration of global inputs, industrial experience, and ethical conduct enables students to develop critical thinking and technical skills.

Opting for MIT AOE from the best private engineering colleges in Pune provides prospective engineers an opportunity to discover, innovate, and meaningfully contribute to technological and scientific growth.

The MIT Academy of Engineering provides undergraduate and graduate degrees in a variety of engineering disciplines, including computer engineering, electronics and telecommunications engineering, mechanical engineering, civil engineering, and information technology.

MIT Academic of Engineering is an Empowered Autonomous Engineering college, affiliated with Savitribai Phule Pune University.

Yes, the MIT Academy of Engineering is associated with Savitribai Phule Pune University.

Students must have finished 10+2 or equivalent with Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics and have a valid score on entrance exams such as JEE Main or MHT-CET.

During the admissions cycle, you can apply online via the official website.

The admissions page includes detailed instructions and forms.

And also you have to fill the CET application form for getting admission in any engineering college.

The MIT Academy of Engineering accepts JEE Main, MHT-CET.

Admissions normally begin in June after entrance exam results are announced.

results.title: MITAOE | Dr. Suraj A. Tat
results.text:
MITAOE
Dr. Suraj A. Tat
Name : Dr. Suraj A. Tat
Designation:Assistant Professor
Qualification: PPhD (Mechanical Engineering), M. TECH (Mechanical - Thermal Engineering), B. TECH (Mechanical Engineering).

PhD in Mechanical Engineering from the Indian Institute of Technology Guwahati in 2025.

M. TECH in Mechanical - Thermal Engineering from the Indian Institute of Technology Ropar in 2019.

B. TECH in Mechanical Engineering from the College of Engineering Pune, Pune in 2016.

Overview of Profile:
Dr. Suraj A. Tat is working as an Assistant Professor in the School of Mechanical Engineering at MIT Academy of Engineering, Pune.

He has completed his PhD from IIT Guwahati in Mechanical Engineering with a research focus on Thermal Energy Storage, Phase Change Materials (PCMs), Thermal Management, and Heat Transfer Augmentation Techniques.

His research integrates both experimental investigations and numerical modeling, with extensive use of COMSOL Multiphysics for simulation and analysis.

His teaching interests include Fluid Mechanics, Heat Transfer, Finite Element Analysis and Materials Engineering.

Fore Front Area of Research: Thermal Energy Storage, Thermal Management of EV Batteries, Heat Recovery Applications, Conjugate Heat Transfer and Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD)

Email id (College only): suraj.tat@mitaoe.ac.in

Recipient of MHRD Fellowship, Department of higher education, Ministry of Human Resource Development (MHRD) for pursuing doctoral research at IIT Guwahati 2019-2024.

Received MHRD Fellowship, Department of higher education, Ministry of Human Resource Development (MHRD) for pursuing Master of Technology at IIT Ropar 2017-2019.

results.title: MITAOE | Ms. Shalaka Hire
results.text:
MITAOE
Ms. Shalaka Hire
Name : Dr. Shalaka Hire
Designation: Assistant Professor
Qualification: BE, M.E., Ph.D
Ph.D. (Civil Engineering) - 2025
M.E. (Construction and Management)
B.E. (Civil Engineering)
Overview of Profile:
Dr. Shalaka Hire completed her PhD research titled "Optimizing Construction Site Safety using Building Information Modeling-enabled Automation" from Symbiosis International University, Pune.

Her research was a joint collaboration with Loughborough University, UK.

Her research aimed to advance construction site safety by integrating BIM with automated rule-checking systems.

Dr. Shalaka Hire works as an Assistant professor at the School of Civil Engineering.

She has solid experience in research in domains such as Building Information Modeling (BIM), Automation, and Site safety.

She graduated in Civil Engineering (2016) from K.K.Wagh Institute of Engineering Education & Research, Nashik, India, and a Master's (2018) from MVP's KBT College of Engineering, Nashik, India.

She has recently submitted her PhD in BIM-enabled Automation for site safety, and her research is an international collaboration between Symbiosis Institute of Technology, SIU, India, and Loughborough University, UK.

She is the recipient of the prestigious Commonwealth Split Site Scholarship, funded by the UK Foreign Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO), which allowed her to conduct research at Loughborough University in the UK for one year.

She engaged in teaching activities for graduate-level civil students in India and postgraduate students at Loughborough University in the UK while conducting her study.

She has also supervised projects in the construction management domain.

She has presented her research at national and international conferences and had it published in a number of prestigious international journals.

Fore Front Area of Research: Construction Management BIM, Automation, and Site Safety.

results.title: MITAOE | Student Internship Program
results.text:
MITAOE
Student Internship Program
SEMESTER LONG INTERNSHIP PROGRAM
1. To help students gain hands-on professional work experience prior to their graduation.
2. To instill qualities like confidence, maturity, responsibility, and social skills necessary for personal and professional growth.
3. To expose students to state-of-the-art facilities, equipment and professional practices in their respective trades.
4. To expose them to the current technological developments relevant to the subject area of training.
5. To create conditions conducive to quest for knowledge and its applicability on the job.
6. To expose students to the engineer’s responsibilities and ethics.
PROCEDURE
1. CR office conveys various industry requirements / internship opportunities to the students.
2. Students apply for internship work to various industries, appear for the interviews and after their selection they submit the approved “Request Letter for Internship in the Industry” letter to the industry.
3. Students join the industry for internship work and report to faculty mentors every fortnight.
4. After successful completion, internship work is evaluated as per the rubrics given in the guidelines.
ELIGIBILITY
1. No live backlogs are allowed
2. CGPA of 8.50 and above (Up-to 7th Semester)
3. If Recruiter/s (MNCs) have offered semester long internship to the selected student/s (before joining the organization after his / her graduation), then in special case recruited students can apply for the same. (Only criteria-I should be satisfied by the student)
4. Only students satisfying the above criteria can be permitted for semester-long internship in any MNCs / R&D laboratories such as DRDO, NCL, NEERI, CDAC and Institutions like IITs/ NITs / International institutes of repute.
SLIP REPORT (ACADEMIC YEAR 2019-20: JANUARY 2020 – JUNE 2020)
1. During the said period, around 100+ students of final year BTECH students have joined their 16 weeks internship program in various industries.
2. Students engaged in around 28 MNCs for SLIP work.
3. After successful completion, Internship work of all the students get evaluated (Presentation + Internship report) by the panel of three members in every school.
4. Based on their performance & industry evaluation reports, grades are given to the students.
5. Positive impact of SLIP has been observed in students thinking and overall personality.
SLIP REPORT (ACADEMIC YEAR 2020-21: JANUARY 2021 – JUNE 2021)
1. During the said period, 245 students of Final Year BTECH have joined their Semester Long Internship Program in various industries. Out of them, 180 students are doing their semester long internship with stipend.
2. Students engaged in 50 (SMEs+MNCs) industries for their SLIP work.
3. After successful completion, Internship work of all the students get evaluated (Presentation + Internship report) by the panel of three members in every school.
4. Based on their performance & industry evaluation reports, grades are given to the students.
5. Positive impact of SLIP has been observed in students thinking and overall personality.

results.title: Semester Long Internship Program
results.text:
Semester Long Internship Program
The MITAOE International Relations Department facilitates a dynamic interchange of knowledge and culture via a transformational semester-long internship programme with top institutions overseas. This programme, which aims to expand perspectives and enhance comprehension, offers students amazing opportunities to get fully immersed in a broad spectrum of academic environments while acquiring hands-on experience in the field they are studying.
The International Semester Long Internship program allows students to immerse themselves in a different academic environment for one full semester, which is designed for those eager to pursue postgraduate studies or explore education in foreign universities.
Pupils can choose to start this life-altering endeavour in their sixth or eighth semester, giving them flexibility in their academic path. Engaging with enterprises and research laboratories, or performing research at universities abroad, provides participants with indispensable insights and experiences that enhance their academic and personal development.
The Selection Process for SLIP
The selection process involves interviews to identify 2 to 4 students who demonstrate exceptional potential and motivation for the Semester Abroad Program. Upon selection, students have the opportunity to attend a full semester abroad, either conducting research projects or studying courses relevant to their field of interest.
Discover the limitless opportunities that await you through the MITAOE International Semester-Long Internship Program/Student Exchange by joining us for this fascinating experience.
Our internship programme extends beyond the confines of traditional academia by giving students the opportunity to interact with new perspectives, adapt to diverse cultural norms, and forge international connections. Students set out on a voyage of discovery that influences their academic and personal growth, equipping them to flourish in an increasingly linked world, with the help of our passionate teachers and international partners.

results.title: Participate in MIT AOE's International Summer Internship Program
section_path: Participate in MIT AOE's International Summer Internship Program
results.text:
Participate in MIT AOE's International Summer Internship Program
International Summer Internship Program (ISIP)
We have signed MOUs with foreign universities. Some of them have given ISIP opportunity to our students.
Every University has different way of conducting ISIP.
In general we follow Project Based Learning (PBL) method wherein the students in small groups of (2 to 3 max) will be doing research-based projects under the mentorship of foreign professor.
They will have very few lectures/ workshops etc(Approximately 6 to 8 lectures per week) and the rest of the time the students will work in groups on their research topic.
ISIP consist of approximately Industry visits (2 to 3), Cultural Tours : 3 to 4, welcome and closing ceremony, certificate distribution
Weekly presentations from the group will be observed and guided by the professor/mentor
ISIP period: June and July every year
Period: 2 weeks to 6 weeks
Level of students: Students from second year and third year of engineering, first year and second year of Arts, Science, Commerce, BBA, second, third year of design, first year of management MBA, MCA, MSc etc
Area of study/project:
Engineering students: Artificial intelligence and Machine learning, internet of things, robotics and computer vision, Electric vehicles, CAD CAM, Structural engineering, racing cars etc
Arts, Science, Commerce, Management students : Doing Business in Asia, Politics, Culture and Media in Southeast Asia.
Management students: Business Modelling
Register here
ISIP 2025

MITAOE
Student Internship Program
OBJECTIVES
1. To provide students possible opportunities to learn, understand and sharpen the real-time technical, managerial and life skills required at the job.
2. To familiarize students to the business environment, which cannot be simulated in the classroom; thus, creating competent professionals for the industry.
3. To help students develop their own job search tools, networking capacities, and interview skills.
4. To expose them to the current technological developments relevant to the subject area of training.
5. To create conditions conducive to quest for knowledge and its applicability on the job.
6. To provide the experience in writing Technical reports/projects.
PROCEDURE
1. CR office conveys various industry requirements / internship opportunities to the students.
2. Students apply for internship work to various industries, appear for the interviews and after their selection they submit the approved “Request Letter for Internship in the Industry” letter to the industry.
3. Students join the industry for internship work and report to faculty mentors every fortnight.
4. After successful completion, internship work is evaluated as per the rubrics given in the guidelines.

results.title: Student Clubs - MIT
results.text:
Student Clubs - MIT
At MITAOE, we are committed to fostering an inclusive and dynamic environment where every student has the opportunity to learn, grow, and make a meaningful impact. Our student clubs play a central role in this mission by offering a wide range of engaging activities designed to enhance both personal and academic development.
From hands-on workshops and insightful seminars to lively social events and impactful community outreach programs, MITAOE's student clubs provide an array of experiences that cater to diverse interests. These initiatives are designed to help students cultivate essential skills, expand their knowledge, and build lasting connections.
Goal
Our goal is to create a safe, healthy, and supportive atmosphere where students can thrive in all aspects of their lives. At MITAOE, we believe in nurturing not just intellectual growth, but also the technical, physical, emotional, and ethical development of our students. By promoting a holistic approach, we aim to help our students become well-rounded individuals who are prepared to contribute meaningfully to society and their communities

results.title: MITAOE | Autosports
results.text:
Team Niyudrath Karting is an auto sports club of the college. Its prime objective is to build a GoKart abiding by the rules of the competition and competing with the teams from all over India in various ways i.e., Design, Business Plan, Sales Plan, Cross Pad, Acceleration, Endurance, Tug of War, etc.
AWARDS AND ACHIEVEMENT
Overall Champions i.e. AIR 1
1st in Cross Pad
1st in Design
2nd in Endurance
3rd in Acceleration
2nd in Business Plan
Future Award
EVENTS PARTICIPATED
Indian Karting Race Season 5, 2020. Dated 21st to 25th January 2020, At Radharaman Institute and RPM Circuit. EVENTS ORGANIZED
Imperial Society of Innovative Engineers (ISIE). SALIENT FEATURES
Team Niyudrath Karting thrives the members to learn about new things about cars and about its parts. It makes them work together and build their own Kart which can compete in National level. It teaches the spirit of team efforts and individual progress. This club focuses on developing skills and practicing software’s required in core industries. Also gives chances to every individual to enhance leadership qualities. Moreover it helps to apply theoretical knowledge into applications in real life.

Google Developer Student Club

results.title: Admissions at MIT Academy of Engineering
section_path: Admissions at MIT Academy of Engineering
results.text:
Admissions at MIT Academy of Engineering
Admissions at MIT Academy of Engineering (MIT AOE)
Choosing where to study engineering can feel like a big decision.

While reputation and location do matter, most students also want a college that is fair and easy to apply to.

For many across Maharashtra and other states, MIT Academy of Engineering (MIT AOE) in Pune fits that picture.

It’s recognised not just for academics but for how clearly it handles admissions.

Each year, thousands look to secure a seat at MIT AOE.

To do that, you need to understand the full system.

Knowing what to prepare, when to act, and which forms to fill can make all the difference.

The institutions admission process here follows Maharashtra’s centralised rules.

If you are applying from within the state or outside it, the steps are the same.

This guide walks you through what you’ll need, from documents to entrance tests.

What is the Admission Process?

Admissions to MIT AOE are done through the state-run Centralised Admission Process (CAP) conducted by the Maharashtra State Common Entrance Test Cell.

This ensures all students are considered on the same grounds.

Here’s how the enrolment process works:
Step
Activity
Mode
1
Register on CET Cell Portal
Online
2
Upload required documents
Online
3
Fill out preferences for colleges & branches
Online
4
Document verification
E-Scrutiny
5
Merit list declared
Online
6
Provisional allotment
Online
7
Accept seat & pay admission fees
Online
8
Report to the allotted institute
In-person
Allotment is strictly merit-based admission, and seats are offered based on ranking, preferences, and category rules.

What Are the Eligibility Criteria for Admission?
Minimum eligibility includes:
Should have passed their 10+2 level studies or equivalent, ensuring both Physics and Mathematics were part of the core curriculum.

One additional subject from Chemistry / Biology / Biotechnology / Technical Vocational.

At least 45% marks for the General category (40% for reserved categories) in aggregate.

Must be an Indian national.

Candidates applying under the All India quota must meet the same conditions, and their scores will be considered separately under the relevant quota.

Which Entrance Exams Are Accepted for Admission?

To get into engineering in Maharashtra, most students sit the MHT-CET — short for Maharashtra Health and Technical Common Entrance Test.

This is the main exam accepted for first-year engineering courses at MIT AOE.

There are two exam routes you can use, depending on the quota you’re applying under:
MHT-CET – required for students applying through the Maharashtra State quota or Open Category
JEE Main – accepted for those applying under the All India quota or through institute-level seats
Your exam score is an important part of the process.

When you fill out your application, you’ll need to enter your marks from either MHT-CET or JEE Main.

Without one of these, you won’t receive a rank in the CAP rounds, which means you won’t be considered for a seat.

How Can Students Apply for Admission to MIT AOE?

The application procedure is fully online and follows a fixed timeline released by the CET Cell.

During the CAP rounds, MIT AOE does not accept direct applications from students — everything must go through the official portal.

Follow these steps to apply for MIT AOE:

Curriculum with emphasis on creativity, communication, collaboration and life skills.(Education 4.0).
One of the Top Engineering Institutions in Pune.
341 + student get placed in 2019-20 academic year.

The Department of Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering is one of the principal academic movers at MIT Academy of Engineering, which facilitates students to specialize in the field of telecommunication. The main objective of the department is to impart strong background of theory and practical in communication Engg. including satellite, wireless and optical, mobile, audio and video etc. The UG program is accredited by NBA (National Board of Accreditation) and ISO 9001:2008 certified.
The program offered is well known for its applied nature including a strong laboratory component and considerable project work. The degree represents an excellent combination of basic engineering science, electronics and communication theory and practice, communication skills and engineering management.

Student Clubs - MIT
At MITAOE, we are committed to fostering an inclusive and dynamic environment where every student has the opportunity to learn, grow, and make a meaningful impact. Our student clubs play a central role in this mission by offering a wide range of engaging activities designed to enhance both personal and academic development.
From hands-on workshops and insightful seminars to lively social events and impactful community outreach programs, MITAOE's student clubs provide an array of experiences that cater to diverse interests. These initiatives are designed to help students cultivate essential skills, expand their knowledge, and build lasting connections.
Goal
Our goal is to create a safe, healthy, and supportive atmosphere where students can thrive in all aspects of their lives. At MITAOE, we believe in nurturing not just intellectual growth, but also the technical, physical, emotional, and ethical development of our students. By promoting a holistic approach, we aim to help our students become well-rounded individuals who are prepared to contribute meaningfully to society and their communities

results.title: MITAOE | ROTARY CLUB
results.text:
MITAOE
ROTARY CLUB
WHAT IS ROTARY ?
Paul Harris founder of Rotary Club, founded in the Chicago (United States) on Feb. 23, 1905. Rotary promote health, peace and prosperity in communities across the globe. It unites people from all continents and cultures who take action to deliver real long term solutions to our world’s most persistent issues.
Rotary is made up of three parts :-
I. Local Clubs
II. Rotary International
III. The Rotary Foundaion
What is ROTARACT ?
Rotaract club brings together people from the age 18 and older to exchange their ideas with leaders in the community, it develops leadership qualities and professionalism. In communities worldwide, Rotary and Rotaract members work side by side to take action through service.
SOME GLIMPSES OF ROTARACT CLUB :-
Parent Club RC Pimpri ELITE
AGENDA OF ROTARACT
Rotary club Meetings are held weekly with focus being on fellowship. That said, there are plenty of news updates and discussion that every member should be kept aware of, and a great way to organiz meetings is with a formal Rotary agenda.
BENEFITS OF ROTARACT FOR MITAOE
This is the first year of Rotaract in MITAOE, as it is an International Organization, there are many benefits for student members.
Personality Growth
Good communication skills
Increase in self confidence
Social interaction
Placement opportunities
Economic knowledge
Leadership Qualities
Interaction with many business tycoons and many industrialists
Decision Making Power
Internship opportunities
FIRST PROGRAM CONDUCTED IN MITAOE
On 21st of April 2022, Rotary Club of Pimpri Elite organized session on “Introduction to Rotary Club and it’s benefits as an Student Engagement Program”. Around 70 Students have participated in the session from various streams and classes across. Session was taken by Mr. Ashok D. Shinde (Secretary of Rotary Club of Pimpri Elite). The Introductory speech was given by Mr.Ravindra Bhave Sir (President of Rotary Club of Pimpri Elite). Faculty coordinator Dr . N.B.Totla hosted and coordinated this session.
CONSIDERATION
We have received Rotaract Club of MIT Academy of Engineering consideration, and is now a member of ROTARY INTERNATIONAL Which is sponsored by Rotary Club of Pimpri Elite.
CERTIFICATE OF ORGANIZATION
TENTATIVE INSTALLATION CEREMONY DATE
By considering over all conditions Installation Ceremony for Rotaract Club of MIT Academy of Engineering will be conducted in first /second week of August .
REPORT ON STUDENT ENGAGEMENT PROGRAM
